items.title: Public Finance and Accountability (Scotland) Act 2000
items.description: An Act of the Scottish Parliament to make provision about public resources and finances and, for the purposes of section 70 of the Scotland Act 1998, about accountability for their use; and for connected purposes

items.title: National Parks (Scotland) Act 2000
items.description: An Act of the Scottish Parliament to make provision for National Parks.

items.title: Regulation of Investigatory Powers (Scotland) Act 2000
items.description: An Act of the Scottish Parliament to regulate surveillance and the use of covert human intelligence sources.

items.title: Sea Fisheries (Shellfish) Amendment (Scotland) Act 2000
items.description: An Act of the Scottish Parliament to amend section 7(4) of the Sea Fisheries (Shellfish) Act 1967 in respect of rights of several fishery.

items.title: Budget (Scotland) Act 2000
items.description: An Act of the Scottish Parliament to make provision, for financial year 2000/01, for payments out of the Scottish Consolidated Fund and the application of sums otherwise payable into the Fund, for the maximum amount of relevant expenditure for the purposes of section 94(5) of the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1973 (c. 65) and the maximum amounts of borrowing by certain statutory bodies; to make provision, for financial year 2001/02, for payments out of the Fund on a temporary basis; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Census (Amendment) (Scotland) Act 2000
items.description: An Act of the Scottish Parliament to amend the Census Act 1920 to enable particulars about religion to be gathered.

items.title: Adults with Incapacity (Scotland) Act 2000
items.description: An Act of the Scottish Parliament to make provision as to the property, financial affairs and personal welfare of adults who are incapable by reason of mental disorder or inability to communicate; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Abolition of Feudal Tenure etc. (Scotland) Act 2000
items.description: An Act of the Scottish Parliament to abolish the feudal system of land tenure; to abolish a related system of land tenure; to make new provision as respects the ownership of land; to make consequential provision for the extinction and recovery of feuduties and of certain other perpetual periodical payments and for the extinction by prescription of any obligation to pay redemption money under the Land Tenure Reform (Scotland) Act 1974; to make further provision as respects real burdens affecting land; to provide for the disentailment of land; to discharge all rights of irritancy held by superiors; to abolish the obligation of thirlage; to prohibit with certain exceptions the granting of leases over land for periods exceeding 175 years; to make new provision as respects conveyancing; to enable firms with separate personality to own land; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Standards in Scotland’s Schools etc. Act 2000
items.description: An Act of the Scottish Parliament to make further provision as respects school education, the welfare of pupils attending independent schools and corporal punishment of pupils for whom school education is provided; to make further provision as respects School Boards; to make further provision as respects the functions, constitution and structure of the General Teaching Council for Scotland; to abolish the committee known as the Scottish Joint Negotiating Committee for School Education; to make further provision relating to the inspection of institutions within the higher education sector which educate and train persons to be, or persons who are, teachers in schools; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Ethical Standards in Public Life etc. (Scotland) Act 2000
items.description: An Act of the Scottish Parliament to establish a framework for securing the observance of high standards of conduct by councillors and other persons holding public appointments; and to repeal section 2A of the Local Government Act 1986 and make provision as to how councils are to exercise functions which relate principally to children.

items.title: Education and Training (Scotland) Act 2000
items.description: An Act of the Scottish Parliament to make provision for the payment of grants in respect of the education and training of certain individuals.

items.title: Bail, Judicial Appointments etc. (Scotland) Act 2000
items.description: An Act of the Scottish Parliament to change the law about bail; to enable alteration in the number of judges in the Inner House of the Court of Session; to abolish temporary sheriffs and create a new kind of sheriff; to change the law about justices of the peace; and to abolish district court prosecutions brought on behalf of or by local authorities.

items.title: Financial Assistance for Political Parties Act (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: An Act to provide for the making of payments to political parties for the purpose of assisting members of the Northern Ireland Assembly who are connected with such parties to perform their Assembly duties.

items.title: Appropriation Act (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: An Act to authorise the issue out of the Consolidated Fund of certain sums for the service of the year ending on 31st March 2001; to appropriate those sums for specified purposes and authorise other sums to be applied as appropriations in aid for those purposes; to authorise the Department of Finance and Personnel to borrow on the credit of the appropriated sums; and to repeal certain Appropriation Orders.

items.title: Allowances to Members of the Assembly Act (Northern Ireland) 2000 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to make provision for the payment of allowances to or in respect of persons who have been members of the Northern Ireland Assembly.

items.title: Child Support, Pensions and Social Security Act (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: An Act to amend the law relating to child support; to amend the law relating to occupational and personal pensions; to amend the law relating to social security benefits and social security administration; to amend Part III of the Family Law Reform (Northern Ireland) Order 1977 and Part V of the Matrimonial and Family Proceedings (Northern Ireland) Order 1989; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Weights and Measures (Amendment) Act (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: An Act to amend the Weights and Measures (Northern Ireland) Order 1981 to allow self-verification of weighing or measuring equipment, testing by official EEA testers and pre-test stamping.

items.title: The Equality (Disability, etc.) (Northern Ireland) Order 2000
items.description: This Order confers new powers in respect of discrimination by reason of disability on the Equality Commission for Northern Ireland. This Order also provides for the appointment of additional Commissioners of that Commission, amends the reporting period of that Commission and amends the transitional and saving provisions of the Fair Employment and Treatment (Northern Ireland) Order 1998.

items.title: The Flags (Northern Ireland) Order 2000
items.description: This Order confers power on the Secretary of State to regulate the flying of flags at government buildings.

items.title: The Appropriation (Northern Ireland) Order 2000
items.description: This Order authorises the issue out of the Consolidated Fund of Northern Ireland of a further sum for the year ending on 31st March 2000 and of sums on account for the year ending on 31st March 2001 and appropriates those sums for specified services in Northern Ireland. It also authorises the application of certain further sums as appropriations in aid for the year ending on 31st March 2000 and reduces certain sums already authorised to be applied as appropriations in aid.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Benefit (Information from Landlords and Agents) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 and further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the Housing Benefit Regulations”).

items.title: Rabies (Importation of Dogs, Cats and Other Mammals) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order further amends the Rabies (Importation of Dogs, Cats and Other Mammals) Order (Northern Ireland) 1977 by permitting the free movement between England and Northern Ireland of pet cats and pet dogs which have been brought into England under the provisions of the Travel Scheme (Pilot Arrangements) (England) Order 1999.

items.title: Asbestos (Licensing) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Asbestos (Licensing) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1984 (“the 1984 Regulations”).

items.title: The Planning (Control of Major-Accident Hazards) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations are concerned with the implementation of Article 12 of Council Directive 96/82/EC (O.J. No. L10, 14.1.1997, p. 13) on the control of major-accident hazards involving dangerous substances.

items.title: Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1989 (“the principal Regulations”), which provide for remission and repayment of certain charges which would otherwise be payable under the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 and for the payment by the Department of travelling expenses incurred in attending a hospital.

items.title: The Income Support (General) (Standard Interest Rate Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
The Social Security (Maternity Allowance) (Earnings) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations provide for the payments which are or are to be treated as earnings for the purpose of calculating entitlement to State Maternity Allowance.

items.title: The Social Security (Incapacity for Work) (Miscellaneous Amendments No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations make amendments in consequence of the coming into operation of the Welfare Reform and Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1999 (“the Order”).

items.title: The Social Security (Overlapping Benefits) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Overlapping Benefits) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1979 by altering the amount of the adjustments of benefits payable under the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 by reference to the new rates of child benefit payable and provide for the commencement of the adjustment to coincide with the Social Security Benefits Up-rating (Northern Ireland) Order 2000.

items.title: The Occupational and Personal Pension Schemes (Penalties) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations are made under the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993 and the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”).

items.title: The Potatoes Originating in Egypt (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement Commission Decision 99/842/EC amending Decision 96/301/EC and authorising member States temporarily to take emergency measures against the dissemination of Pseudomonas solanacearum (Smith) Smith as regards Egypt (O.J. No. L.326, 18.12.98, p. 68) by amending the definition of “the Decision” in the Potatoes Originating in Egypt Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1998 (“the 1998 Regulations”) (regulation 2(2)). Decision 99/842/EC makes minor modifications to the requirements applying to potatoes imported into member States from Egypt.

items.title: The Social Security (Approved Work) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend—

items.title: Poultry Breeding Flocks and Hatcheries Scheme (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order amends the Poultry Breeding Flocks and Hatcheries Scheme Order (Northern Ireland) 1994 (“the 1994 Order”). It removes turkeys, ducks and geese from the scope of the 1994 Order. It also removes the requirement for taking blood samples from domestic fowl in breeding flocks for serological testing for Salmonella pullorum.

items.title: Planning (General Development) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends the Planning (General Development) Order (Northern Ireland) 1993 (“the 1993 Order”) in relation to certain permitted development rights. Where such rights apply no specific application for planning permission is required.

items.title: The Personal Social Services (Direct Payments) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: The Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 (as amended by the Personal Social Services (Direct Payments) (Northern Ireland) Order 1996) enables the Department of Health, Social Services and Public Safety to make direct payments to persons in respect of their securing the provision of personal social services.

items.title: Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (S.R. 1996 No. 542) by altering the conditions under which a person suffering from insulin-treated diabetes may be granted a licence to drive vehicles in sub-categories C1 and C1 + E (8.25 tonnes).

items.title: Rates (Amendment) (1998 Order) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 1st April 2000 Article 6 of the Rates (Amendment) (Northern Ireland) Order 1998. The 1998 Order is now fully in operation from that date, the rest of its provisions having come into operation on 1st April 1999 in accordance with Article 1(2): see the Rates (Amendment) (1998 Order) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 1999 (S.R. 1999 No. 58 (c. 7)).

items.title: Transport of Dangerous Goods (Safety Advisers) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement, as respects Northern Ireland, Council Directive 96/35/EC on the appointment and vocational qualification of safety advisers for the transport of dangerous goods by road, rail and inland waterway (O.J. No. L145, 19.6.96, p. 10).

items.title: Control of Substances Hazardous to Health Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations re-make, with modifications, the Control of Substances Hazardous to Health Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (S.R. 1995 No. 51) (“the 1995 Regulations”) as amended. The 1995 Regulations imposed duties on employers to protect employees and other persons who may be exposed to substances hazardous to health and also imposed certain duties on employees concerning their own protection from such exposure (regulations 1 to 17 and Schedules 1 to 8).

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) (Repayment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations are the first to be made under Article 3 of the Education (Student Support) (Northern Ireland) Order 1998 dealing with the repayment of student loans. The Regulations apply to students who started higher education courses in September 1998 or later. Students who began their courses before then, and certain students who began their courses after then (for example gap year students), remain subject to the previous system of mortgage style loans for maintenance under the Education (Student Loans) (Northern Ireland) Order 1990.

items.title: Employment Relations (1999 Order) (Commencement No. 3 and Transitional Provision) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 24th April 2000, Article 18 of, and Schedule 5 to, the Employment Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1999. These provisions amend the Employment Rights (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (“the 1996 Order”) to provide that an employee is regarded as unfairly dismissed for the purposes of Part XI of that Order if dismissed, in certain circumstances, because he or she took part in official industrial action. The provisions also make consequential amendments to Article 137 of the 1996 Order (unfair selection for redundancy). Article 3 of the Order provides that the changes apply only to the dismissal of employees who start to take industrial action beginning on or after 24th April 2000.

items.title: The Income-Related Benefits and Jobseeker’s Allowance (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996.

items.title: The Plant Health (Amendment No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order implements in relation to Northern Ireland Council Directive 98/57/EC, on the control of Ralstonia solanacearum (Smith) Yabuuchi et al. (O.J. No. L.235, 21.8.98, p. 1), by amending the Plant Health Order (Northern Ireland) 1993. This Order—

items.title: Cereal Seeds (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement Commission Directive 99/8/EC (O.J. No. L50, 26.2.99, p. 26) and Commission Directive 99/54/EC (O.J. No. L142, 5.6.99, p. 30) amending Council Directive 66/402/EEC (O.J. No. L125, 11.7.66, p. 2309/66) (O.J./SE 1965-66, p. 143) on the marketing of cereal seed by amending the Cereal Seeds Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1994 as amended (“the 1994 Regulations”).

items.title: Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 by increasing fees in respect of driving licences. The fees for licences for which fees are charged are increased by an average of 57%. Licences that are issued free of charge remain unchanged.

items.title: Motor Vehicles (Third-Party Risks) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Third-Party Risks) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1994 as follows—

items.title: The Welfare Reform and Pensions (1999 Order) (Commencement No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of the following provisions of the Welfare Reform and Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1999 (“the Order”)—

items.title: Pneumoconiosis, etc., (Workers' Compensation) (Payment of Claims) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: Under the Pneumoconiosis, etc., (Workers' Compensation) (Northern Ireland) Order 1979 (“the Order”) lump sum payments may be made to certain persons disabled by a disease to which the Order applies, or to dependants of persons who were so disabled immediately before they died.

items.title: The Social Security (National Insurance Number Information: Exemption) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Child Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1979 in order to provide that section 11(1A) of the Social Security Administration (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (“the Administration Act”) (as inserted by Article 66 of the Welfare Reform and Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1999 (“the Order”)) shall not apply to claims for child benefit made in respect of children in the care of voluntary organisations (regulation 2).

items.title: Public Service Vehicles (Conditions of Fitness, Equipment and Use) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Service Vehicles (Conditions of Fitness, Equipment and Use) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 by substituting Schedule 6, which shows the plate to be displayed by a taxi with a taximeter and licensed for public hire. Previously the plate indicated that a vehicle was licensed to carry 6 persons, with a permitted variant of 5 persons. The new plate indicates that a vehicle is licensed to carry 6 persons, with permitted variants of 5 persons, 7 persons or 8 persons.

items.title: Seed Potatoes (Crop Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations increase the fees payable to the Department of Agriculture and Rural Development in respect of the certification of seed potato crops arising under the Seed Potatoes Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1981 (regulation 2 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Children (1995 Order) (Commencement No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation on 15th May 2000 of provisions of the Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the 1995 Order”) enabling the making of Regulations. It also provides for the coming into operation on 4th September 2000 of Articles 149(1)(i), 176 and 178 (in so far as it relates to Article 176) of the 1995 Order which relate to children accommodated in schools.

items.title: The Equality (Disability, etc.) (2000 Order) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 25th April 2000 certain provisions of Part II of the Equality (Disability, etc.) (Northern Ireland) Order 2000.

items.title: The Pensions on Divorce etc. (Provision of Information) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations set out the requirements imposed on a person responsible for a pension arrangement with respect to the supply of information to members and their spouses (or former spouses) in relation to pensions on divorce, separation or nullity.

items.title: The Pensions on Divorce etc. (Charging) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations set out the circumstances in which a person responsible for a pension arrangement may recover charges in respect of the provision of information in connection with pensions on divorce, separation or nullity, complying with an earmarking or attachment order, or in connection with pension sharing activity.

items.title: The Pension Sharing (Valuation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations specify the types of pension rights which are not subject to pension sharing, and make provision for the calculation and verification of cash equivalents for the purpose of creating pension debits and credits.

items.title: The Pension Sharing (Implementation and Discharge of Liability) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations provide for the circumstances in which the implementation period may be extended, postponed or in which it may cease, and how a person responsible for a pension arrangement may discharge his liability in respect of a pension credit for the purposes of Schedule 5 to the Welfare Reform and Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1999 (“the 1999 Order”). They also provide for the circumstances concerning the failure by the trustees or managers of an occupational pension scheme to discharge their liability within the implementation period or, where an application is made for leave to appeal against a pension sharing order out of time, for the postponement or cessation of the implementation period.

items.title: The Pension Sharing (Pension Credit Benefit) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations provide for the requirements relating to, and the indexation of, pension credit benefit.

items.title: The Pension Sharing (Safeguarded Rights) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations provide for the treatment of safeguarded rights by pension arrangements once a pension has been shared.

items.title: Motor Cars (Driving Instruction) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Cars (Driving Instruction) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1991 by increasing fees as follows—

items.title: Public Service Vehicles (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Service Vehicles Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1985. The principal amendments are as follows:

items.title: Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) (Test Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 by increasing fees for driving tests as follows—

items.title: Motor Vehicle Testing (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles Testing Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the 1995 Regulations”). Those Regulations make provision for certain motor vehicles to be examined by persons authorised by the Department and for test certificates to be issued for vehicles that are found to meet certain requirements.

items.title: Goods Vehicles (Testing) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Goods Vehicles (Testing) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 as follows—

items.title: Razor Shells (Prohibition of Fishing) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Razor Shells (Prohibition of Fishing) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1998 which prohibit, subject to certain exemptions, any boat from fishing for razor shells by means of any type of dredge or suction dredge or retaining on board razor shells caught by any type of dredge or suction dredge, in waters within Dundrum Bay.

items.title: The Undersized Whiting (Revocation) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order revokes the Undersized Whiting Order (Northern Ireland) 1993 which prescribed a minimum size for whiting.

items.title: The Local Government (General Grant) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order—

items.title: Motor Cycles (Eye Protectors) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations prescribe certain types of eye protectors as authorised for use by persons driving or riding on a motor bicycle. The eye protectors are so prescribed by reference to—

items.title: Land Registration (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the rules governing the procedure of the Land Registry, the Land Registration Rules (Northern Ireland) 1994 (S.R. 1994 No. 424) (“the Land Registration Rules”) to give effect to Article 42(4)(h) to (j) of the Property (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 (S.I. 1997/1174 (N.I. 8)), which contains provisions for the determination of disputes by the Registrar of Titles.

items.title: Land Registry (Fees) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: This Order prescribes the fees to be taken in the Land Registry for the purposes of the Land Registration Act (Northern Ireland) 1970 and the manner in which those fees are to be paid.

items.title: Census Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the taking of a census in Northern Ireland on 29th April 2001. The Order specifies the persons by whom and with respect to whom the census returns are to be made and sets out the particulars to be stated in the returns.

items.title: Road Vehicles Lighting Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations revoke and consolidate with amendments the Road Vehicles Lighting Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 and Road Vehicles Lighting (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997. The amendments are as follows—

items.title: The Transport of Explosives (Safety Advisers) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 96/35/EC on the appointment and vocational qualification of safety advisers in so far as it relates to explosives by road, rail and inland waterway (O.J. No. L145, 19.6.96, p. 10).

items.title: Education (Student Support) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations, made under the Education (Student Support) (Northern Ireland) Order 1998, come into operation on 20th June 2000. They further amend the Education (Student Support) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 (“the Student Support Regulations”).

items.title: Local Government Pension Scheme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These regulations retitle and consolidate, with amendments, the provisions of the Local Government (Superannuation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (S.R. 1992 No. 547), (“the 1992 regulations”) as amended.

items.title: Local Government Pension Scheme (Management and Investment of Funds) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations provide for the management and investment of the superannuation funds managed by the Northern Ireland Local Government Officers' Superannuation Committee. The provisions were formerly dealt with in the Local Government (Superannuation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the 1992 Regulations”) at regulations P2 and P3. The 1992 Regulations have been consolidated in the Local Government Pension Scheme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (S.R. No. 177).

items.title: Inspection of Premises, Children and Records (Children Accommodated in Schools) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the inspection of schools which provide accommodation for children. They provide for persons authorised by an authority for the purposes of Article 176(4) of the Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the Order”) to inspect school premises, the children accommodated therein and the records maintained in respect of such children. Authorised persons may enter schools accommodating children in exercise of the power conferred by Article 176(4) of the Order.

items.title: The Social Security (Claims and Payments) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: Regulation 2 of these Regulations amends paragraph 6 of Schedule 8B to the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 by reducing from £0·60 to £0·54 the fee which qualifying lenders pay for the purpose of defraying administrative expenses incurred by the Department for Social Development in making payments in respect of mortgage interest direct to those lenders.

items.title: The Welfare Reform and Pensions (1999 Order) (Commencement No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: Article 2 of this Order provides for the coming into operation of the following provisions of the Welfare Reform and Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1999 (“the Order”) as follows—

items.title: The Social Security Revaluation of Earnings Factors Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order corresponds to an Order (S.I. 2000/1365) made by the Secretary of State for Social Security under sections 148(3) and (4) and 189(5) of the Social Security Administration Act 1992. It directs that the earnings factors relevant to the calculation of the additional pension in the rate of any long-term benefit or of any guaranteed minimum pension, or to any other calculation required under Part III of the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993, are to be increased for the tax years specified in the Schedule to this Order by the percentage of their amount specified in that Schedule. The percentage for the tax year 1999-2000 is 6·3 per cent., and those for earlier tax years have been increased so that the earnings factors for those years are revalued at 1999-2000 earnings levels.

items.title: Horse Racing (Charges on Bookmakers) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: Article 9(1) of the Horse Racing (Northern Ireland) Order 1990 provides for charges to be paid to the Department of Agriculture and Rural Development by persons who intend to apply for bookmakers' and bookmaking office licences.

items.title: Food (Animal Products from Belgium) (Emergency Control) (Revocation) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order revokes the Food (Animal Products from Belgium) (Emergency Control) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000 (S.R. 2000 No. 72—“the revoked Order”) and implements, in relation to food, Commission Decision 2000/301/EC repealing the protective measures with regard to contamination by dioxins of certain products of porcine and poultry origin intended for human or animal consumption (O.J. No. L97, 19.4.2000, p. 16). (The protective measures concerned were contained in Commission Decision 1999/788/EC (O.J. No. L310, 4.12.1999, p. 62) as amended by Commission Decision 2000/150/EC (O.J. No. L50, 23.2.2000, p. 25) and were implemented, in relation to food, by the revoked Order).

items.title: Tetrachloroethylene in Olive Oil (Revocation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Tetrachloroethylene in Olive Oil Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1989, as amended, which made provision for the enforcement of Article 1 of Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 1860/88 on the establishment of special marketing standards for olive oil (OJ No. L166, 1.7.88, p. 16) which set a limit for tetrachloroethylene, a volatile halogenated solvent. That Commission Regulation was revoked by Commission Regulation 3009/91 (OJ No. L286, 16.10.91, p. 19), and limits on volatile halogenated solvents were instead included in Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 2568/91 (OJ No. L248, 5.9.91, p. 1) enforcement of which was provided for in the Olive Oil (Marketing Standards) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993 (S.R. 1993 No. 9).

items.title: Medical Food Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations implement Commission Directive 1999/21/EC on dietary foods for special medical purposes (O.J. No. L91, 7.4.99, p. 29).

items.title: Colours in Food (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Colours in Food Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 by bringing up to date a reference to Commission Directive 95/45/EC laying down specific criteria concerning colours for use in foodstuffs (O.J. No. L226, 22.9.95, p. 1) so as to cover its amendment by Commission Directive 1999/75/EC (O.J. No. L206, 5.8.99, p. 19), which changed the specification for “E160a(i) Mixed Carotenes”, and by bringing up to date references to the 1996 Regulations in other Regulations.

items.title: Genetically Modified and Novel Foods (Labelling) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations make continued provision for the enforcement of—

items.title: Food Protection (Shellfish Emergency Prohibitions) (Strangford Lough) (Revocation) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order revokes the Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Strangford Lough) Order (Northern Ireland) 1999 (“the 1999 Order”) and the amendment threto. The 1999 Order, as amended, contained emergency prohibitions on various activities in order to prevent human consumption of Queen scallops or food which is derived from Queen scallops originating in waters in Strangford Lough. The effect of this Order is to lift these prohibitions.

items.title: Meat (Enhanced Enforcement Powers) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Regulations specified respectively in paragraphs 2, 13, 25, 35 and 44 of this note.

items.title: Employment Rights (Time Off for Study or Training) (1998 Order) (Commencement) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 1st September 2000 the provisions of the Employment Rights (Time Off for Study or Training) (Northern Ireland) Order 1998. The 1998 Order amends the Employment Rights (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 to provide for a right of certain young employees to time off to study or train for qualifications.

items.title: Employment Rights (Time Off for Study or Training) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under Article 91A of the Employment Rights (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (the “1996 Order”), set out the standard of achievement which is prescribed for the purposes of Article 91A(1)(c) of the 1996 Order (regulation 3). An employee who has not attained the standard of achievement has a right to take time off for study or training leading to a relevant qualification.

items.title: The Ionising Radiation (Medical Exposure) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations partially implement, as respects Northern Ireland, Council Directive 97/43/Euratom (O.J. No. L180. 9.797. p. 22) laying down basic measures for the health protection of individuals against dangers of ionising radiation in relation to medical exposure. The Regulations impose duties on those responsible for administering ionising radiation to protect persons undergoing medical exposure whether as part of their own medical diagnosis or treatment or as part of occupational health surveillance, health screening, voluntary participation in research or medico-legal procedures.

items.title: The Social Security (Attendance Allowance and Disability Living Allowance) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Attendance Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the Attendance Allowance Regulations”) and the Social Security (Disability Living Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the Disability Living Allowance Regulations”).

items.title: The Income Support (General) (Standard Interest Rate Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 3 to the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 with respect to the standard rate of interest applicable to a loan which qualifies for income support. The rate is increased from 6·92 per cent. to 7·44 per cent. (regulation 2). The Regulations also revoke, with a saving provision, the Income Support (General) (Standard Interest Rate Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 which made a previous amendment to the standard rate of interest (regulation 3).

items.title: The Jobseeker’s Allowance (Amendment No. 3) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (“the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations”).

items.title: The Census Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: The Regulations provide for the detailed arrangements necessary for the conduct of the census directed to be taken by the Census Order (Northern Ireland) 2000.

items.title: Fair Employment (Specification of Public Authorities) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Fair Employment (Specification of Public Authorities) Order (Northern Ireland) 1989 as amended. It specifies a number of persons or bodies as public authorities for certain purposes under Part VII of the Fair Employment and Treatment (Northern Ireland) Order 1998 (“the 1998 Order”) and provides for the persons who are to be treated for such purposes as the employees of some of those authorities.

items.title: The Social Security (Education Maintenance Allowance Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996.

items.title: Crabs and Lobsters (Minimum Size) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: Section 127(1) of the Fisheries (Northern Ireland) Act 1966 (“the 1966 Act”) prohibits the bringing to land, landing, sale or offering or exposure for sale or the possession for the purposes of sale of sea fish of a description prescribed by regulations where those fish are smaller than the minimum size so prescribed. For the purposes of this sub-section, this Order prescribes the minimum size for lobsters, male spider crabs and velvet crabs (Article 3 and Schedule 2).

items.title: Motor Hackney Carriage (Newry) (Amendment) Bye-Laws (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Bye-Laws amend the Bye-Laws relating to Motor Hackney Carriages standing or plying for hire made by Newry Urban District Council on 8th April 1968 by reducing the number of taxi stands in Merchants Quay from 8 to 4.

items.title: The Welfare Reform and Pensions (1999 Order) (Commencement No. 5) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of the following provisions of the Welfare Reform and Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1999 on 1st December 2000:—

items.title: The Divorce etc. (Pensions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations make provision pursuant to orders, including those made after proceedings overseas, for ancillary relief in proceedings for divorce, judicial separation and nullity of marriage which relate to the pension rights of a party to the marriage. In particular, they provide for—

items.title: The Criminal Evidence (1999 Order) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order commences as from 1st June 2000 the provisions of the Criminal Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1999 in so far as they relate to the making of court rules to bring the provision of the Order into effect. It also provides that the restrictions on the use of evidence from computer records are removed (Article 37).

items.title: The Legal Aid in Criminal Proceedings (Costs) (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the Legal Aid in Criminal Proceedings (Costs) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1992 so as to alter the date after which certain work may be remunerated at discretionary instead of prescribed rates from 30th June 2000 to 30th June 2001.

items.title: Education (Student Support) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations, which come into operation on 20th May 2000, provide for support for students attending higher education courses in respect of academic years beginning on or after 1st September 2000.

items.title: The Social Security (Industrial Injuries) (Prescribed Diseases) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Industrial Injuries) (Prescribed Diseases) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1986 by—

items.title: The Social Security and Child Support (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Industrial Injuries) (Prescribed Diseases) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1986 (“the Prescribed Diseases Regulations”), the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the Claims and Payments Regulations”), the Child Support (Maintenance Assessment Procedure) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the Maintenance Assessment Procedure Regulations”), the Child Support Departure Direction and Consequential Amendments Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (“the Departure Direction Regulations”) and the Social Security and Child Support (Decisions and Appeals) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 (“the Decisions and Appeals Regulations”).

items.title: Game Birds Preservation Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order prohibits, except where exemption is granted, the killing or taking of partridge, red-legged partridges and hen pheasants during the normal open season for game birds. It also restricts dealings in partridge, red-legged partridges or hen pheasants and prohibits dealings in grouse.

items.title: General Medical Services (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the General Medical Services Regulations (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the terms on which doctors provide general medical services under the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972.

items.title: The Criminal Evidence (1999 Order) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order commences as from 14th June 2000 Article 39(1) of the Criminal Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1999 which provides for the making of court rules to bring the provisions of the Order into effect. The first part of the rule making powers was commenced on 1st June 2000 by the Criminal Evidence (1999 Order) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000.

items.title: Part-time Workers (Prevention of Less Favourable Treatment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations come into operation on 1st July 2000 and implement Directive 97/81/EC (normally referred to as the Part-time Work Directive) as extended to the United Kingdom by Directive 98/23/EC.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) (Amendment No. 3) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend provisions in the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 in respect of the calculation of average weekly income. In particular they provide that claimants in receipt of either working families' tax credit or disabled person’s tax credit can have that credit taken into account in the calculation of the child care costs disregard applicable in their case.

items.title: The Income Support (General) and Jobseeker’s Allowance (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996.

items.title: Anthrax (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order amends the Anthrax Order (Northern Ireland) 1969, by—

items.title: Anthrax (Vaccination) Scheme Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order introduces a Scheme for keeping animals in Northern Ireland free from anthrax and for the control of that disease.

items.title: The Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (Extension of Group A Offences) Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends Article 38(2)(c) of the Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 by extending the Group A offences to which Part III of the Criminal (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 applies so as to include various offences contained in Part II of the Forgery and Counterfeiting Act 1981. Part III of the 1996 Order makes provision about the jurisdiction of courts in Northern Ireland in relation to certain offences of dishonesty and blackmail set out in Article 38 of that Order.

items.title: The Crown Court (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the Crown Court Rules (Northern Ireland) 1979 to:

items.title: Fair Employment (Monitoring) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the provisions of the Fair Employment (Monitoring) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 (“the principal Regulations”) which inter alia require employers to provide certain information about the composition of their workforce in monitoring returns.

items.title: Local Government (Superannuation) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These regulations amend the Local Government (Superannuation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Environmental Protection (Disposal of Polychlorinated Biphenyls and other Dangerous Substances) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations relate to Council Directive 96/59/EC (“the Directive”) on the disposal of polychlorinated biphenyls and polychlorinated terphenyls (“PCBs”). They are for the purpose of implementing the provisions of the Directive which require decontamination or disposal of PCBs and equipment containing them and the associated provisions for inventories, labelling and monitoring. They are additional to the existing relevant requirements of the waste management licensing regime under the Waste and Contaminated Land (Northern Ireland) Order 1997.

items.title: Animal Feedingstuffs from Belgium (Control) (Revocation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Animal Feedingstuffs from Belgium (Control) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (“the revoked Regulations”) and implement, in relation to products for animal feeding, Commission Decision 2000/301/EC repealing the protective measures with regard to contamination by dioxins of certain products of porcine or poultry origin intended for human or animal consumption (O.J. No. L97, 19.04.2000, p. 16). (The protective measures concerned were contained in Commission Decision 1999/788/EC (O.J. No. L310, 4.12.1999, p. 62) as amended by Commission Decision 2000/150/EC (O.J. No. L50, 23.2.2000, p. 25) and were implemented, in relation to products for animal feeding, by the revoked Regulations).

items.title: Processed Cereal-based Foods and Baby Foods for Infants and Young Children (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Processed Cereal-based Foods and Baby Foods for Infants and Young Children Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 in implementation of Commission Directive 1999/39/EC amending Directive 96/5/EC on processed cereal-based foods and baby foods for infants and young children.

items.title: Infant Formula and Follow-on Formula (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Infant Formula and Follow-on Formula Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 in implementation of Commission Directive 1999/50/EC amending Directive 91/321/EC on infant formulae and follow-on formulae.

items.title: Plant Protection Products (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Plant Protection Products Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (S.R. 1995 No. 371), as amended by S.R. 1997 No. 471, S.R. 1997 No. 507 and S.R. 1999 No. 282 (“the principal Regulations”) which implement in Northern Ireland Council Directive 91/414/EEC (O.J. No. L230, 19.8.91, p. 1 to be read with the Corrigenda published in O.J. No. L170, 25.6.92, p. 40) concerning the placing of plant protection products on the market (“the Directive”), as amended.

items.title: Industrial Training Levy (Construction Industry) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to proposals submitted by the Construction Industry Training Board to the Department of Higher and Further Education, Training and Employment for the imposition of a further levy upon employers in the construction industry for the purpose of raising money towards the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Social Security (Students Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996.

items.title: The Social Security (Students and Income-Related Benefits Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the Housing Benefit Regulations”), the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the Income Support Regulations”) and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (“the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations”).

items.title: The Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) (Amendment No. 2) 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) 1980 so as to:

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations, which come into operation on 1st August 2000, amend the Education (Student Loans) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1998 (“the principal Regulations”). The principal Regulations govern loans made under the Education (Student Loans) (Northern Ireland) Order 1990, which are mortgage style repayment loans. Such loans are for the most part made to students who began their courses before 1st August 1998. Loans under the Student Support (Northern Ireland) Order 1998, repayable through the tax system, are made to students beginning their courses on or after 1st August 1998.

items.title: The Social Security (Personal Allowances for Children Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 and the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Republic of Korea) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order makes provision for the modification of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 and the Social Security Administration (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 so as to give effect in Northern Ireland to the Convention on Social Security between the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Government of the Republic of Korea. The provisions relate to liability for social security insurance contributions arising in respect of employment.

items.title: The Insolvency (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This version of the The Insolvency (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Foyle Area (Rivers Finn and Foyle Angling Permits) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations which replace the Foyle Area (Rivers Finn and Foyle Angling Permits) Regulations 1989 and the Foyle Area (Rivers Finn and Foyle Angling Permits) (Amendment) Regulations 1990 prohibit a person fishing with rod and line for salmon or trout in the waters of the River Finn described in Schedule 1 and the waters of the River Foyle described in Schedule 2 unless he is the holder of a River Finn angling permit or, as the case may be, a River Foyle angling permit. The Regulations provide for the issue of such permits.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) (Amendment No. 4) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987.

items.title: Students Awards (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which come into operation on 24th August 2000, amend the Students Awards Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 (“the Students Awards Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Therapeutic Earnings Limits) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (General Benefit) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1984, the Social Security (Incapacity Benefit) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1994 and the Social Security (Incapacity for Work) (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 to take into account the increase in the national minimum wage.

items.title: Animals and Animal Products (Import and Export) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-enact with changes the Animals and Animal Products (Import and Export) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (S.R. 1995 No. 52) as amended by the Animals and Animal Products (Import and Export) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (S.R. 1997 No. 87).

items.title: Education (Student Support) (2000 Regulations) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend Part II of Schedule 3 (parental contribution) to the Education (Student Support) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000. They insert a new paragraph (aa) in paragraph 6(2) of Schedule 3 to provide, in the case of a student whose course begins before 1st September 2000, for the gross amount of loan interest on which relief is given under the Income Tax Acts to be deducted in determining the parent’s residual income for the purposes of ascertaining the parental contribution under the Regulations (regulation 2(3)).

items.title: The Jobseeker’s Allowance (Amendment No. 4) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996.

items.title: The Social Fund (Winter Fuel Payment and Maternity and Funeral Expenses (General)) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Fund Winter Fuel Payment Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 and the Social Fund (Maternity and Funeral Expenses (General)) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987.

items.title: The Social Security (Bereavement Benefits Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 and the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987.

items.title: Industrial Tribunals (Constitution and Rules of Procedure) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Industrial Tribunals (Constitution and Rules of Procedure) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (“the Principal Regulations”). The amendments relate to the amount of information to be placed by the Secretary in the Register, which is open to public inspection, in relation in particular to applications and appeals.

items.title: The Stakeholder Pension Schemes Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations are made principally under Articles 3, 4, 5 and 9 of the Welfare Reform and Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1999 (“the 1999 Order”), and other provisions listed in Schedule 1 to the Regulations, to make provision in connection with stakeholder pension schemes introduced by Part II of that Order. Apart from Part IV (which provides for the obligations of employers), the Regulations come into operation on 1st October 2000.

items.title: The Social Security (Attendance Allowance and Disability Living Allowance) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Attendance Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 and the Social Security (Disability Living Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992.

items.title: Students Awards (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations correct many drafting errors in regulations 11(1) and 18(4) of the Students Awards Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 and shall come into operation on 31st August 2000.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) (Amendment No. 5) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the Housing Benefit Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Payments on account, Overpayments and Recovery) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 16 of the Social Security (Payments on account, Overpayments and Recovery) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988 to provide for the higher rate of deduction to recover overpayments from income support or jobseeker’s allowance to be applied to a person who, after caution, makes an admission of deception or fraud or agrees to pay a penalty as an alternative to prosecution.

items.title: The Prison and Young Offenders Centre (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the Prison and Young Offenders Centre Rules (Northern Ireland) 1995 (the principal rules) so as to remove the disciplinary powers delegated by the Secretary of State to the boards of visitors in respect of adjudications presently held under Rules 40 and 125 of the principal rules and provide for the consequential amendment of the provisions of the principal rules relating to adjudication procedures.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) (Amendment No. 6) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987.

items.title: Class Sizes in Primary Schools Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: Article 16 of the Education (Northern Ireland) Order 1998 provides for a statutory limit to be prescribed by regulations on the number of pupils who may be in any class of pupils in Key Stage 1 of their compulsory education. It also provides for the Department to set out in those regulations the matters which education and library boards must have regard to when making exceptions to the class size limit.

items.title: Welfare of Farmed Animals Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace with amendments the Welfare of Livestock Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 and the Welfare of Livestock (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1998.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Human Rights Act 1998) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This version of the The Magistrates' Courts (Human Rights Act 1998) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates' Courts Rules (Northern Ireland) 1984, in consequence of the Human Rights Act 1998, so as to provide that a finding by a county court on appeal that the making of the order appealed against has infringed the appellant’s rights under Article 5 of the European Convention on Human Rights shall be certified on the notice of appeal and recorded in the Order Book.

items.title: County Court (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the County Court Rules (Northern Ireland) 1981 so as to insert into Order 52 a new Part VII which prescribes the practice and procedure in relation to proceedings in a county court under the Human Rights Act 1998.

items.title: Waterside, Coleraine Cycle/Pedestrian Bridge Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the construction of a cycle/pedestrian bridge over the navigable waters of the River Bann from a point on the west bank approximately 7 metres north of the Bann Bridge (Ordnance Survey map co-ordinates 284527 and 432435) to a point on the east bank approximately 7 metres north of the Bann Bridge (Ordnance Survey map co-ordinates 284603 and 432426) in the town of Coleraine, County of Londonderry.

items.title: Meat (Disease Control) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations give effect in part to the provisions of the following Council Directives relating to disease control:

items.title: Salaries (Assembly Ombudsman and Commissioner for Complaints) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: This Order provides for an increase in the annual salaries payable to the Assembly Ombudsman for Northern Ireland and the Northern Ireland Commissioner for Complaints.

items.title: Transport of Animals and Poultry (Cleansing and Disinfection) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces, with changes, the Transport of Animals and Poultry (Cleansing and Disinfection) (No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997. The Order implements paragraph 8 of Chapter I of the Annex to Council Directive 91/628/EEC on the protection of animals during transport (O.J. No. L340, 11.12.91, p. 17). It also implements Article 12.1(a) of Council Directive 64/432/EEC on health problems affecting intra-Community trade in bovine animals and swine (this Directive was consolidated in the Annex to Council Directive 97/12/EC, O.J. No. L109, 25.4.97, p. 1).

items.title: Specified Risk Material (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations give effect in part to Article 3.1 of Commission Decision 2000/418/EC regulating the use of material presenting risks as regards transmissible spongiform encephalopathies (O.J No. L158, 30.6.2000, p. 76). That provision requires member States to ensure that certain material from cattle, sheep and goats (“specified risk material”) is removed and destroyed in accordance with the Annex to the Decision. These Regulations amend the Specified Risk Material Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (S.R. 1997 No. 552, as amended).

items.title: Education (Student Support) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which come into operation on 6th October 2000, further amend the Education (Student Support) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (“the Student Support Regulations”).

items.title: Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 18 of the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 so as to authorise the Chief Fire Officer of the Fire Authority for Northern Ireland to conduct practical driving tests for persons employed, or to be employed, by him to drive motor vehicles.

items.title: Marketing of Potatoes (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations increase the fee payable for the inspection or examination of ware potatoes from £3·35 per tonne or part of a tonne to £3·40 per tonne or part of a tonne.

items.title: Seed Potatoes (Tuber and Label Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Seed Potatoes (Tuber and Label Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995.

items.title: The Social Security and Child Support (Decisions and Appeals) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security and Child Support (Decisions and Appeals) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999.

items.title: Magistrates' Courts (Domestic Proceedings) (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates' Courts (Domestic Proceedings) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1996 by substituting a new Form D3.

items.title: The Sheep Annual Premium (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Sheep Annual Premium Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the 1992 Regulations”) to provide for a shorter period in which to apply for sheep annual premium in respect of any marketing year as defined by Article 3(3) of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2467/98 (O.J. No. L312, 10.11.98, p. 1). A producer shall deliver his application on or after 27th November and not later than 29th December in the preceding calendar year. Previously the producer was requested to deliver his application in the period from 8th December 1999 to 19th January 2000.

items.title: The Education (1998 Order) (Commencement No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 19th October 2000 Articles 34 to 41 of and Schedule 1 to the Education (Northern Ireland) Order 1998 which make provision for the constitution and functions of the General Teaching Council for Northern Ireland. Those provisions enable the Department, inter alia, to appoint the first registrar of the Council and to make regulations, as to the eligibility of persons for registration and the form and manner in which the register is kept. The Order also makes some consequential amendments which relate to the establishment of the Council.

items.title: Food Irradiation Provisions Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations give effect to the provisions of—Directive 1999/2/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on the approximation of the laws of the member States concerning foods and food ingredients treated with ionising radiation (O.J. No. L66, 13.3.99, p. 16); andDirective 1999/3/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on the establishment of a Community list of foods and food ingredients treated with ionising radiation (O.J. No. L66, 13.3.99, p. 24).

items.title: Level Crossing (Antrim) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the provision and maintenance of remotely operated manually controlled barriers in lieu of gates at Antrim railway level crossing. Section 47 of the Railways Clauses Consolidation Act 1845 (which requires the railway undertaking to provide gates and gate-keepers, section 6 of the Railways Clauses Act 1863 (requirements as to lodges, etc.) and any other statutory provision imposing requirements to the same or similar effect, shall not apply to the crossing whilst this Order remains in force.

items.title: Level Crossing (Kingsmoss East)) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the provision and maintenance of a system of automatic barriers in lieu of manually operated gates at Kingsmoss East railway crossing. Section 47 of the Railways Clauses Consolidation Act 1845 (which requires the railway undertaking to provide gates and gate-keepers, section 6 of the Railways Clauses Act 1863 (requirements as to lodges, etc.) and any other statutory provision imposing requirements to the same or similar effect, shall not apply to the crossing whilst this Order remains in force.

items.title: Level Crossing (Kingsmoss West) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the provision and maintenance of a system of automatic barriers in lieu of manually operated gates at Kingsmoss West railway crossing. Section 47 of the Railways Clauses Consolidation Act 1845 (which requires the railway undertaking to provide gates and gate-keepers), section 6 of the Railways Clauses Act 1863 (requirements as to lodges, etc.) and any other statutory provision imposing requirements to the same or similar effect, shall not apply to the crossing whilst this Order remains in force.

items.title: Level Crossing (Ballymartin) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the provision and maintenance of a system of automatic barriers in lieu of manually operated gates at Ballymartin railway crossing. Section 47 of the Railways Clauses Consolidation Act 1845 (which requires the railway undertaking to provide gates and gate-keepers, section 6 of the Railways Clauses Act 1863 (requirements as to lodges, etc.) and any other statutory provision imposing requirements to the same or similar effect, shall not apply to the crossing whilst this Order remains in force.

items.title: Level Crossing (Kilmakee) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the provision and maintenance of a system of automatic barriers in lieu of manually operated gates at Kilmakee railway crossing. Section 47 of the Railways Clauses Consolidation Act 1845 (which requires the railway undertaking to provide gates and gate-keepers), section 6 of the Railways Clauses Act 1863 (requirements as to lodges, etc.) and any other statutory provision imposing requirements to the same or similar effect, shall not apply to the crossing whilst this Order remains in force.

items.title: Level Crossing (Kingsbog) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the provision and maintenance of a system of automatic barriers in lieu of manually operated gates at Kingsbog railway crossing. Section 47 of the Railways Clauses Consolidation Act 1845 (which requires the railway undertaking to provide gates and gate-keepers), section 6 of the Railways Clauses Act 1863 (requirements as to lodges, etc.) and any other statutory provision imposing requirements to the same or similar effect, shall not apply to the crossing whilst this Order remains in force.

items.title: Students Awards Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which come into operation on 20th October and have effect on and from 1st September 2000, revoke and replace, with amendments the Students Awards Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 as amended.

items.title: Compulsory Registration of Title Order (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: This Order declares the majority of County Down to be a compulsory registration area under the Land Registration (Northern Ireland) Act 1970 upon the commencement of this Order on1st February 2001. The type of property transaction which triggers a compulsory registration within that area is defined by entry 2 of Schedule 2 to the aforesaid Act.

items.title: The Police and Criminal Evidence (Application to Police Ombudsman) Order (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: Article 3 of the Order applies to officers of the Police Ombudsman, subject to specified modifications, provisions of the Police and Criminal Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1989, contained in Schedule 1 to the Order, relating to investigation of offences and detention of persons by the police. Schedule 2 to the Order substitutes for police ranks, the equivalent of Ombudsman Officer grades.

items.title: The Royal Ulster Constabulary (Conduct) Regulations 2000
items.description: These regulations set out the procedures to be followed where an officer’s conduct is suspected of falling below the standard set out in the Code of Conduct, contained in Schedule 4.

items.title: Royal Ulster Constabulary (Unsatisfactory Performance) Regulations 2000
items.description: These regulations make provision with respect to the assessment of the performance of members of the Royal Ulster Constabulary and the Royal Ulster Constabulary Reserve and establish procedures for cases in which a member who is not performing satisfactorily may be dealt with by way of requirement to resign, reduction in rank or warning. Parts II and III of the regulations make provision for interviewing members where it is considered that their performance is unsatisfactory and identifying areas of the performance which require improvement. Part IV makes provision for the holding of a hearing (called an unsatisfactory performance hearing) where it is considered that a member’s performance has not improved. If the chairman of the hearing finds that the member’s performance has been unsatisfactory he may require the member concerned to resign, order reduction in his rank or issue a warning. Part V makes provision for an appeal to the Chief Constable.

items.title: Royal Ulster Constabulary (Appeals) Regulations 2000
items.description: These regulations make provision as to the procedure on appeals to a police appeals tribunal.

items.title: Royal Ulster Constabulary (Complaints etc.) Regulations 2000
items.description: These regulations are made under Part VII of the Police (Northern Ireland) Act 1998 and provide procedures for a new police complaints system in Northern Ireland whereby there will be a Police Ombudsman who will have independent control of the police complaints system. The regulations provide procedures to ensure that the Ombudsman shall exercise his powers in such a manner to secure the efficiency, effectiveness and independence of the police complaints system; and the confidence of the public and of members of the police force in that system.

items.title: Royal Ulster Constabulary (Complaints) (Informal Resolution) Regulations 2000
items.description: These regulations provide for the informal resolution of complaints against police officers in accordance with Section 53 of the Police (Northern Ireland) Act 1998. Informal resolution is intended to provide a flexible and simple procedure for dealing with complaints of a minor nature which would otherwise attract the extensive and rigorous process of formal investigation. This is a procedure, which in many circumstances, is the most appropriate way to deal with a complaint to the satisfaction of the complainant.

items.title: Royal Ulster Constabulary (Conduct) (Senior Officer) Regulations 2000
items.description: These regulations replace Part III and Schedule 3 of the Royal Ulster Constabulary (Discipline and Disciplinary Appeals) Regulations 1988 and come into operation on 6th November 2000. They take account of the new provisions made by the Police (Northern Ireland) Act 1998 for complaints by members of the public against police officers.

items.title: The Social Fund Winter Fuel Payment (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Fund Winter Fuel Payment Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000. Unregistered homes are excluded from the definition of “residential care” for the purposes of entitlement to a winter fuel payment; however, a person living in such a home who has a preserved right under regulation 19 (applicable amounts for persons in homes for persons in need and nursing homes) of the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 is treated as living in a residential care home (regulation 2(2)). A person is not disentitled to a winter fuel payment even though he is a partner of a person entitled to such a payment where the 2 people do not live in the same household (regulation 2(3)).

items.title: Disabled Persons (Badges for Motor Vehicles) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These regulations further amend the Disabled Persons (Badges for Motor Vehicles) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993.

items.title: The Statutory Maternity Pay (General) (Modification and Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations modify the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (“the Act”) as it relates to the conditions of entitlement to statutory maternity pay and further amend the Statutory Maternity Pay (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the Statutory Maternity Pay Regulations”).

items.title: Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Reduction of Grant) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 (revoked)
