These are reserved matters in terms of Schedule 5 to the Scotland Act 1998 (c. 46). Such investigations in Scotland should therefore be conducted under the direction of the Advocate General for Scotland rather than the Lord Advocate, and these Rules make the necessary change to the Merchant Shipping (Formal Investigations) Rules 1985.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Inquiries Procedure) (England) Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules regulate the procedure to be followed in connection with local inquiries in England held by the Secretary of State before he determines applications referred to him, or appeals made to him, in relation to planning permission, listed building consent and consent for the demolition of unlisted buildings in conservation areas (known as “conservation area consent”). Provision is also made for the participation of the Mayor of London.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning Appeals (Determination by Inspectors) (Inquiries Procedure) (England) Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules regulate the procedure to be followed in connection with local inquiries in England held by inspectors appointed by the Secretary of State to determine appeals made to him in relation to planning permission, listed building consent and consent for the demolition of unlisted buildings in conservation areas (known as “conservation area consent”). Provision is also made for the participation of the Mayor of London.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Hearings Procedure) (England) Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules regulate the procedure to be followed for hearings in England caused by the Secretary of State to be held before he or an inspector determines appeals made to him in relation to planning permission, listed building consent and consent for the demolition of unlisted buildings in conservation areas (“conservation area consent”) on or after 1st August 2000. The Rules come into force on 1st August 2000.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (General Development Procedure) (England) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends articles 22 and 23 of the Town and Country Planning (General Development Procedure) Order 1995 (“the 1995 Order”).

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Appeals) (Written Representations Procedure) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: Section 78 of the Town and Country Planning Act 1990 confers a right of appeal against certain planning decisions of the local planning authority and against cases where the local planning authority fails to notify such a planning decision within the prescribed periods.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Vine Telecom Networks Limited) Order 2000
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Vine Telecom Networks Limited a licence on 22 November 1999 under secton 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 31st January 2000. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Vine Telecom Networks Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 29th February 2000.

items.title: The Charities (Royal Medical Foundation of Epsom College) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to a Scheme of the Charity Commissioners for the Charity known as Epsom College, formerly the Royal Medical Benevolent College, to be known in future as the Royal Medical Foundation of Epsom College (the name under which it already has Royal patronage).

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) Order 2000
items.description: This order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, safety zones having a radius of 500 metres from the specified point, around each installation specified in the Schedule to this order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29)(article 2).

items.title: The Pet Travel Scheme (Pilot Arrangements) (England) (Amendment) (No. 3) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, which revokes the Pet Travel Scheme (Pilot Arrangements) (England) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order (S.I. 2000/1564), makes a minor amendment to article 2(3) of the Pet Travel Scheme (Pilot Arrangements) (England) (Amendment) Order 2000 (S.I. 2000/1298).

items.title: The A501 Trunk Road (Westminster) Red Route (Clearway) Traffic Order 2000
items.description: This Order is based on a comprehensive model Order produced by the Traffic Director for London to assist understanding of the regulations, and to assist future consolidation of Orders relating to priority routes.

items.title: The A3220 Trunk Road (Hammersmith & Fulham) Red Route (Clearway) Traffic Order 2000
items.description: This Order is based on a comprehensive model Order produced by the Traffic Director for London to assist understanding of the regulations, and to assist future consolidation of Orders relating to priority routes.

items.title: The A40 Trunk Road (Hammersmith & Fulham, Kensington & Chelsea and Westminster) Red Route (Clearway) Traffic Order 2000
items.description: This Order is based on a comprehensive model Order produced by the Traffic Director for London to assist understanding of the regulations, and to assist future consolidation of Orders relating to priority routes.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical Services) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (General Medical Services) Regulations 1992 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the terms on which doctors provide general medical services under the National Health Service Act 1977.

items.title: The Explosive Substances (Hazard Information) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000
items.description: These Regulations apply to any substance which is dangerous for supply and to which the Explosives Acts (Northern Ireland) 1875 to 1970 apply.

items.title: The A13 Trunk Road (Tower Hamlets) Red Route Traffic Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The A13 Trunk Road (Tower Hamlets) Red Route Traffic Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Greater London Authority Act 1999 (Commencement No. 7, Transitional Provisions and Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: Article 2 of this Order brings into force on 3rd July 2000 the provisions of the Greater London Authority Act 1999 which relate to the Metropolitan Police Authority and members of the metropolitan police force in so far as they are not already in force. It also brings into force various consequential amendments and repeals subject to the transitional provisions set out in the Schedule to the Order. Where amendments and repeals relate to the functions of the Receiver for the Metropolitan Police District, the provisions are commenced only in relation to his functions for police purposes.

items.title: The London Cab Order 1934 (Modification) Order 2000
items.description: This Order modifies the London Cab Order 1934 in consequence of the transfer to Transport for London of the functions of the Secretary of State for the Environment, Transport and the Regions in relation to the licensing of London cabs.

items.title: The Bovines and Bovine Products (Trade) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Bovines and Bovine Products (Trade) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/1103, as amended—“the Principal Regulations”) insofar as they extend to England. The Principal Regulations give effect to Commission Decisions 98/692/EC (OJ No. L328, 4.12.98, p. 28) and 98/564/EC (OJ No. L273, 9.10.98. p. 37) which amended Council Decision 98/256/EC (OJ No. L113, 15.4.98, p. 32).

items.title: The Dangerous Substances and Preparations (Nickel) (Safety) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations implement European Parliament and Council Directive 94/27/EC (OJ No. L 188, 22.7.94, p.1).

items.title: The East London and The City Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the establishment Order of the East London and The City Mental Health National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust established by Order under Part I of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990, by inserting a new article 4A to provide that the trust is to be regarded as having a significant teaching commitment.

items.title: The A316 Trunk Road (Hanworth Road, Country Way and Great Chertsey Road, Hounslow and Spelthorne) (Speed Limits) Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The A316 Trunk Road (Hanworth Road, Country Way and Great Chertsey Road, Hounslow and Spelthorne) (Speed Limits) Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Refund of Tax) (No. 3) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 17th July 2000, specifies Transport for London for the purposes of section 33 of the Value Added Tax Act 1994 (“the VAT Act”). Transport for London is the body corporate established by section 154 of the Greater London Authority Act 1999.

items.title: The Animals and Animal Products (Import and Export) (England and Wales) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-enact with changes the Animals and Animal Products (Import and Export) Regulations 1998 (S.I. 1998/190).

items.title: The Railways (Interoperability) (Notified Bodies) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 96/48 of 23rd July 1996 on the interoperability of the trans-European high speed rail system.

items.title: The Borough of North Warwickshire (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the borough of North Warwickshire. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 1st May 2003.

items.title: The Borough of Rugby (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the borough of Rugby. The changes, with the exception of those made by articles 4 and 5, have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The District of Warwick (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the district of Warwick. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 1st May 2003.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/1774, as amended by S.I. 1999/3243; “the principal Regulations”) by altering fees set out in Schedule 2 to the principal Regulations to be paid in relation to certain wireless telegraphy licences granted under section 1 of the Wireless Telegraphy Act 1949 (c. 54) (regulation 2(d)).

items.title: The Cosmetic Products (Safety) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Cosmetic Products (Safety) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/2925) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Immigration and Asylum Act 1999 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provisions) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999 (the Act).

items.title: The Local Authority (Stocks and Bonds) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Authority (Stocks and Bonds) Regulations 1974 to enable stock and bonds which have been transferable through the medium of the CGO Service (referred to below) by means of an exempt transfer (referred to below) to be transferred instead in accordance with the Uncertificated Securities Regulations 1995 by means of a relevant system (within the meaning of the 1995 Regulations) operated by a person currently only CRESTCo Limited, who has been approved under the 1995 Regulations Under the 1995 Regulations title to stock may be evidenced otherwise than by a certificate and transferred without a written instrument.

items.title: The Government Stock (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Government Stock Regulations 1965 to enable stock which has been transferable through the medium of the CGO Service (referred to below) by means of an exempt transfer (referred to below) to be transferred instead in accordance with the Uncertificated Securities Regulations 1995 by means of a relevant system (within the meaning of the 1995 Regulations) operated by a person, currently only CRESTCo Limited, who has been approved under the 1995 Regulations. Under the 1995 Regulations title to stock may be evidenced otherwise than by a certificate and transferred without a written instrument.

items.title: The Uncertificated Securities (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Uncertificated Securities Regulations 1995 to allow for securities which have been transferable through the medium of the CGO Service being transferred instead in accordance with those Regulations by means of a relevant system operated by an Operator, currently only CRESTCo Limited.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Fees)(Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Merchant Shipping (Fees) Regulations 1996. They increase from £70 to £72 the main hourly charge for services performed by the Maritime and Coastguard Agency (the successor body to the Marine Safety Agency) for services under Part I of the Schedule to those Regulations; make increases in the hourly rates charged for services in relation to `Radio Rules' (i.e. the statutory instruments relating to radio installations and navigational equipment on merchant ships and fishing vessels); and make equivalent changes for certain charges for additional services, under Part XII as follows:old feenew feeNo feeNo fee£70£72£140£144£70£72£35£36£70£72.

items.title: The Rutland Sixth Form College, Oakham (Dissolution) Order 2000
items.description: This Order dissolves with effect from 1st August 2000 the further education corporation established to conduct Rutland Sixth Form College, Oakham. It provides for the transfer of its property, rights and liabilities to Tresham Institute, Kettering, and secures the rights of its employees by applying section 26(2) to (4) of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992.

items.title: The Feedingstuffs (Zootechnical Products) and Medicated Feedingstuffs (Amendment) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Feedingstuffs (Zootechnical Products) Regulations 1999 by introducing a Community method of analysis for the determination of lasalocid sodium in feedingstuffs, in implementation, in relation to zootechnical additives and products with zootechnical additives in them, of Commission Directive 1999/76/EC (OJ No. L207, 6.8.1999, p. 13).

items.title: The Friendly Societies (Insurance Business) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend Part V of the Friendly Societies (Insurance Business) Regulations 1994 (“the 1994 Regulations”) which is concerned with the determination of a friendly society’s liabilities, this being relevant to the margin of solvency required to be maintained by a society in accordance with section 48 of the Friendly Societies Act 1992.

items.title: The British Wool Marketing Scheme (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The British Wool Marketing Scheme (Amendment) Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Telecommunications (Licence Modification) (Satellite Operator Licences) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the telecommunications licences granted to the licensees specified in Schedule 1 to the Regulations for the provision of satellite telecommunication services. The Schedules to the licences are replaced by the Schedules set out in the Regulations. The amendments are being made to these licences as part of the implementation in the United Kingdom of Directive 97/13/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on a common framework for general authorisations and individual licences in the field of telecommunications (O.J. No. L199, 26.7.97, p.32), which requires that conditions in all telecommunications licences of a similar type should be harmonised, except where objectively justified in particular instances.

items.title: The Telecommunications (Licence Modification) (Regional Public Access Mobile Radio Operator Licences) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the telecommunications licences granted to the licensees specified in Schedule 1 for the provision in certain areas specified in each licence of telecommunication services consisting in the conveyance by means of telecommunication systems of messages using wireless telegraphy. The Schedules to the licences are replaced by the Schedules set out in the Regulations. The amendments are being made to these licences as part of the implementation in the United Kingdom of Directive 97/13/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on a common framework for general authorisations and individual licences in the field of telecommunications (O.J. No. L199, 26.7.97, p.32), which requires that conditions in all telecommunications licences of a similar type should be harmonised, except where objectively justified in particular instances.

items.title: The Telecommunications (Licence Modifications) (Amendment) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations correct certain minor errors contained in the Telecommunications (Licence Modification) (Standard Schedules) Regulations 1999, the Telecommunications (Licence Modification) (British Telecommunications plc) Regulations 1999 and the Telecommunications (Licence Modification) (Fixed Voice Telephony and International Facilities Operator Licences) Regulations 1999 which modified telecommunications licences held by public telecommunications operators.

items.title: The Telecommunications (Licence Modification) (Mobile Data Operator Licences) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the telecommunications licences granted to Cognito Limited, D.I.C.E. Ltd, Hutchison Mobile Data Limited, Ram Mobile Data Limited and Securicor Datatrak Limited for the provision of telecommunication services consisting in the conveyance by means of telecommunication systems of data messages using wireless telegraphy. The Schedules to the licences are replaced by the Schedules set out in the Regulations. The amendments are being made to these licences as part of the implementation in the United Kingdom of Directive 97/13/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on a common framework for general authorisations and individual licences in the field of telecommunications (O.J. No. L199, 26.7.97, p.32), which requires that conditions in all telecommunications licences of a similar type should be harmonised, except where objectively justified in particular instances.

items.title: The Telecommunications (Licence Modification) (Paging Operator Licences) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the telecommunications licences granted to the licensees specified in Schedule 1 to the Regulations for the provision of paging services. The Schedules to the licences are replaced by the Schedules set out in the Regulations. The amendments are being made to these licences as part of the implementation in the United Kingdom of Directive 97/13/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on a common framework for general authorisations and individual licences in the field of telecommunications (O.J. No. L199, 26.7.97, p.32), which requires that conditions in all telecommunications licences of a similar type should be harmonised, except where objectively justified in particular instances.

items.title: The West Norfolk Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the West Norfolk Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the area for which the trust is established (article 3), its operational date (article 5) and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 6). It makes provision for the membership of the trust (article 4) and for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 7). It also contains a restriction on the exercise of functions by the trust (article 8).

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (Borough of Reading)Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates the Borough of Reading, excluding those parts of the M4 Motorway and its slip roads situated within the Borough, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1999 (Commencement No. 3) (England) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into force on 3rd July 2000, those provisions in Part I of the Local Government Act 1999 (Best Value) which are not already in force. These are the provisions which make the Greater London Authority (GLA) and its functional bodies best value authorities for the purposes of that Part. The GLA’s functional bodies are the London Fire and Emergency Planning Authority, Transport for London, the London Development Agency and the Metropolitan Police Authority.

items.title: The Borough of Shrewsbury and Atcham (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect, subject to the modification detailed below, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the borough of Shrewsbury and Atcham.

items.title: The General Betting Duty (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st August 2000, revoke regulations 9 and 10 of the General Betting Duty Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1963) (“the GBDR”).

items.title: The Superannuation (Admission to Schedule 1 to the Superannuation Act 1972) (No. 2) Order 2000
items.description: Section 1 of the Superannuation Act 1972 (under which the Principal Civil Service Pension Scheme, the Civil Service Additional Voluntary Contribution Scheme and the Civil Service Compensation Scheme have been made) applies to employments and offices listed in Schedule 1 to that Act.

items.title: The Financial Services and Markets (Transitional Provisions) (Designated Date for Certain Self-Regulating Organisations) Order 2000
items.description: Both the Personal Investment Authority Limited and the Investment Management Regulatory Organisation Limited are recognised self-regulating organisations and recognised self-regulating organisations for friendly societies under the Financial Services Act 1986 (c. 60) (the 1986 Act). Schedule 21 to the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 makes transitional provisions concerning the position of such organisations pending the repeal of those provisions of the 1986 Act relating to their recognition and subsequent supervision. Amongst those provisions (which came into force on the passing of the Financial Services and Markets Act on 14th June 2000) are ones which are applicable, from a date to be designated by the Treasury, in relation to an organisation which is a recognised self-regulating organisation or a recognised self-regulating organisation for friendly societies. The relevant provisions have the effect of preventing the recognition of the organisation under the 1986 Act being revoked and of preventing an application for a compliance order being made to the court in respect of the organisation under section 13 of, or paragraph 6 of Schedule 11 to, that Act.

items.title: The Time Off for Public Duties Order 2000
The Football Spectators (Seating) Order 2000 (revoked)
items.description: This Order directs the Football Licensing Authority to include in any licence to admit spectators to the football ground specified in Schedule 1 a condition imposing requirements specified in Schedule 2 as respects the seating of spectators at designated football matches at that premises. “Designated football matches” are those association football matches designated by the Secretary of State, under the powers conferred by section 1(2) of the Football Spectators Act 1989, in the Football Spectators (Designation of Football Matches in England and Wales) Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/1691) and they include all association football matches played at the premises specified in Schedule 1 to this Order.

items.title: The Inheritance Tax (Settled Property Income Yield) Order 2000
items.description: This Order has effect in relation to certain transfers of value taking effect on or after 18th February 2000.

items.title: The Herefordshire Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the Herefordshire Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the area for which the trust is established (article 3), its operational date (article 5) and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 6). It makes provision for the membership of the trust (article 4) and for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 7). It also contains a restriction on the exercise of functions by the trust (article 8).

items.title: The Herefordshire Community Health National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the dissolution on 1st October 2000 of the Herefordshire Community Health National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Education (Funding for Teacher Training) Designation (No. 2) Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates Access to Music Limited, a company limited by shares incorporated under the number 2749258, Association for Science Education, a charitable unincorporated association having the registered charity number 313123, Barnet London Borough Council, Blackburn with Darwen Borough Council, Blackpool Borough Council, City of Bradford Metropolitan District Council, Brent London Borough Council, Bristol City Council, British Association of Advisers and Lecturers in Physical Education, British Institute of Learning Disabilities, a charitable company limited by guarantee having the registered charity number 1019663, Bromley London Borough Council, Croydon London Borough Council, Derby City Council, Derbyshire County Council, Design and Technology Association, a charitable company limited by guarantee having the registered charity number 1062270, Ealing London Borough Council, Greenwich London Borough Council, Hackney London Borough Council, Hamilton Trust, a charitable trust having the registered charity number 1004205, Hammersmith and Fulham London Borough Council, Hampshire County Council, Havering London Borough Council, Hertfordshire County Council, Hillingdon London Borough Council, Hounslow London Borough Council, Kingston-upon-Hull City Council, Knowsley Metropolitan Borough Council, Lambeth London Borough Council, Lancashire County Council, Leicester City Council, Luton Borough Council, Medway Council, Milton Keynes Council, Norfolk County Council, North East Lincolnshire Borough Council, North Lincolnshire Council, North Tyneside Borough Council, Northumberland County Council, North Yorkshire County Council, Nottingham City Council, Peterborough City Council, Council of the Borough and County of the Town of Poole, Redbridge London Borough Council, Redcar and Cleveland Borough Council, Council of the City of Salford, Sandwell Metropolitan Borough Council, Sheffield City Council, Slough Borough Council, Southend-on-Sea Borough Council, Start Educational Services Limited, a company limited by shares having the registration number 2839230, St Helens Metropolitan Borough Council, Council of the City of Stoke-on-Trent, Surrey County Council, Torbay Borough Council, Tower Hamlets London Borough Council, Wandsworth London Borough Council and Wigan Borough Council as institutions eligible for funding under Part I of the Education Act 1994.

items.title: The Hendon College (Dissolution) Order 2000
items.description: This Order dissolves with effect from 1st August 2000 the further education corporation established to conduct Hendon College. It provides for the transfer of its property, rights and liabilities to Barnet College, and secures the rights of its employees by applying section 26(2) to (4) of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992.

items.title: The London Local Authorities (Charges for Stopping Up Orders) Regulations 2000
items.description: Schedule 22 to the Greater London Authority Act 1999 amends Part X of the Town and Country Planning Act 1990 so as to enable orders authorising the stopping up of highways or extinguishing rights to use vehicles on highways to be made by London borough councils and the Common Council of the City of London. Regulation 3 of these Regulations enables councils to impose charges in connection with applications for the making of such orders. Regulation 4 provides for the charges to be payable by the person making the application and regulation 5 makes provision as to the amount of the charges.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Amendment) (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1979 (S.R. 1979 No. 186) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Health Service Medicines (Control of Prices of Specified Generic Medicines) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to the United Kingdom, control the price of certain generic medicines which are sold for the purposes of the national health services in England and Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland (see the definition of “health service” in section 38(6) of the Health Act 1999). They apply only to the medicines specified in the list of controlled prices which is published on the web site at the address http://www.doh.gov.uk/generics. Printed copies of the list are available from the Department of Health, Room 130, Richmond House, 79 Whitehall, London SW1A 2NS. Moreover, the Regulations apply only to medicines in respect of which marketing authorisations have been granted (regulation 2).

items.title: The London Borough of Bromley (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect, subject to the modifications referred to below, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the London borough of Bromley. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The London Borough of Camden (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the London borough of Camden. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The London Borough of Hillingdon (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the London borough of Hillingdon. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The Royal Borough of Kingston upon Thames (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the Royal borough of Kingston upon Thames. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The London Borough of Newham (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the London borough of Newham. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The Rail Vehicle Accessibility (ScotRail Class 170/4 Vehicles) Exemption Order 2000
items.description: This Order authorises the use of specified rail vehicles forming Class 170/4 diesel multiple-units, although they do not conform with certain requirements of the Rail Vehicle Accessibility Regulations 1998 because:

items.title: The Firearms (Amendment) Act 1988(Firearms Consultative Committee) Order 2000
items.description: This Order continues in existence the Firearms Consultative Committee established under section 22(8) of the Firearms (Amendment) Act 1988 for a further period of two years from the date of the expiry of the current period of its existence.

items.title: The Rail Vehicle Accessibility (Anglia Railways Class 170/2 Vehicles) Exemption Order 2000
items.description: This Order authorises the use of specified rail vehicles forming Class 170/2 diesel multiple-units, although they do not conform with certain requirements of the Rail Vehicle Accessibility Regulations 1998 because:

items.title: The Royal Navy Terms of Service (Ratings) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Royal Navy Terms of Service (Ratings) Regulations 1982 (“the 1982 Regulations”).

items.title: The Royal Marines Terms of Service (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Royal Marines Terms of Service Regulations 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”).

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These regulations further amend the Local Authorities (Capital Finance) Regulations 1997. Regulation 3 provides that when an authority uses an amount set aside as provision to meet credit liabilities to pay a premium to a lender on the early repayment of a loan, the credit ceiling shall be increased by the amount so used. Regulation 5 provides that this increase shall be taken into account in determining the non-housing amount in calculating the Minimum Revenue Provision (“MRP”) the authority shall make. Regulation 4 provides that the credit ceiling shall be reduced by the amount of any payment made by the Secretary of State to the Public Works Loan Commissioners (other than a commuted payment under s 157) following a large scale voluntary transfer of houses by the authority. Regulation 6 provides that this reduction in the credit ceiling shall be taken into account in determining the “non-housing reduction” in the calculation of MRP.

items.title: The Channel Tunnel (International Arrangements) (Amendment No. 2) Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the Channel Tunnel (International Arrangements) Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/1813). It modifies paragraph 2A of Schedule 2 to the Immigration Act 1971 (as inserted by paragraph 57 of Schedule 14 to the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999) so that immigration officers will be able to exercise the powers given to them by that paragraph in control zones in France and Belgium, as well as in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Immigration (Control of Entry through Republic of Ireland) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: Article 7 of the Immigration (Leave to Enter and Remain) Order 2000 provides that a person may be given leave to enter the United Kingdom before he arrives in the United Kingdom. Article 13 of that Order provides that a person’s leave to enter or remain in the United Kingdom is not to lapse on his leaving the common travel area.

items.title: The Education (Nutritional Standards for School Lunches) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations introduce nutritional standards for school lunches for registered pupils at maintained nursery schools, community, foundation and voluntary schools and community and foundation special schools.

items.title: The Northern Ireland Act 1998 (Designation of Public Authorities) Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates bodies for the purposes of section 75 of the Northern Ireland Act 1998.

items.title: The Seed Potatoes (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Seed Potatoes Regulations 1991, S.I. 1991/2206, as amended by S.I. 1992/1031, 1993/1878, 1994/2592 and 1997/1474 (the 1991 Regulations) in so far as they extend to England. Similar Regulations to amend the 1991 Regulations in so far as they extend to Wales and Northern Ireland are being considered by the competent authorities in Wales and Northern Ireland and in Scotland new Regulations to replace the 1991 Regulations as respects Scotland have recently been made (S.S.I. 2000/201).

items.title: The Oil and Fibre Plant Seeds (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Oil and Fibre Plant Seeds Regulations 1993, S.I. 1993/2007, as amended by S.I. 1994/1423, 1996/1451, 1997/616 and 1999/1862 (the 1993 Regulations), in so far as they extend to England. Similar Regulations are being made to amend the 1993 Regulations in so far as they extend to Scotland and are being considered by the competent authorities in Wales and Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Health Authorities Act 1995 (Rectification of Transitional Arrangements) Order 2000
items.description: This Order is made under section 44 of the Health Act 1999. It amends the Health Authorities Act 1995 (Transitional Provisions) Order 1996 so as to rectify certain defects in that Order with retrospective effect. The effect of this is that where one Health Authority (“the acting Health Authority”) acted in relation to practitioners providing services under Part II of the National Health Service Act 1977 (“Part II practitioners”), where another Health Authority (“the proper Health Authority”) should have acted, the proper Health Authority will be treated as though it had been the acting one.

items.title: The Vegetable Seeds (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Vegetable Seeds Regulations 1993, S.I. 1993/2008, as amended by S.I. 1996/1452, 1997/616 and 1999/1863 (the 1993 Regulations), in so far as they extend to England. Similar Regulations are being made to amend the 1993 Regulations in so far as they extend to Scotland and are being considered by the competent authorities in Wales and Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Beet Seeds (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Beet Seeds Regulations 1993, S.I. 1993/2006, as amended by S.I. 1997/616 and 1999/1861 (the 1993 Regulations), in so far as they extend to England. Similar Regulations are being made to amend the 1993 Regulations in so far as they extend to Scotland and are being considered by the competent authorities in Wales and Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Fodder Plant Seeds (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Fodder Plant Seeds Regulations 1993, S.I. 1993/2009, as amended by S.I. 1993/2529, 1996/1453, 1997/616 and 1999/1864 (the 1993 Regulations), in so far as they extend to England. Similar Regulations are being made to amend the 1993 Regulations in so far as they extend to Scotland and are being considered by the competent authorities in Wales and Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Cereal Seeds (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Cereal Seeds Regulations 1993, S.I. 1993/2005, as amended by S.I. 1995/1482, 1997/616, 1999/1860 and 1999/2196 (the 1993 Regulations), in so far as they extend to England. Similar Regulations are being made to amend the 1993 Regulations in so far as they extend to Scotland and are being considered by the competent authorities in Wales and Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Prison (Amendment) Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the Prison Rules 1999 (“the 1999 Rules”). Rule 2 of these Rules introduces the Schedule, which sets out the amendments to the 1999 Rules.

items.title: The Young Offender Institution (Amendment) (No. 2) Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the Young Offender Institution Rules 1988 (“the 1988 Rules”). Rule 2 of these Rules introduces the Schedule, which sets out the amendments to the 1988 Rules. They provide for a number of new disciplinary offences relating to racist behaviour by prisoners. The new offences are racially aggravated assault; causing racially aggravated damage to, or destruction of, any part of a young offender institution or any other property, other than the young offender’s own; using threatening, abusive or insulting racist words or behaviour; and displaying, attaching or drawing on any part of a young offender institution, or any other property, threatening, abusive or insulting racist material.

items.title: The A406 Trunk Road (Hanger Lane and Woodville Gardens, Ealing) (Prohibition of Right Turn) Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The A406 Trunk Road (Hanger Lane and Woodville Gardens, Ealing) (Prohibition of Right Turn) Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Consumer Credit (Advertisements and Content of Quotations) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Consumer Credit (Advertisements) Regulations 1989 (“the 1989 Regulations”) and Part II of the Consumer Credit (Content of Quotations) and Consumer Credit (Advertisements) (Amendment) Regulations 1999 (“the 1999 Regulations”).

items.title: Electronic Communications Act 2000 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into force sections 7, 11 and 12 of the Electronic Communications Act 2000 on 25th July 2000. Part I of the Act has not yet been brought into force. All other provisions came into force at the time of Royal Assent.

items.title: The Infant Class Sizes (Admission and Standard Numbers) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations make transitional provisions in connection with the imposition of a limit on the size of infant classes at maintained schools in England under section 1 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998. They apply in relation to the admission of children to such schools for education in infant classes in the 2000–2001 school year.

items.title: The M1 Motorway (Junction 15) (Speed Limit) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations impose a speed limit of 40 miles per hour on a length of the northbound entry slip road linking the A508 with the northbound carriageway of the M1 Motorway, at Junction 15 in the County of Northampton. The length affected runs from the junction of the slip road with the A508 for a distance of 30 metres northward.

items.title: The Legal Aid (Notification of Very High Cost Cases) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations provide that all Very High Cost Cases are to be reported to the Legal Services Commission by the conducting solicitor.

items.title: The Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (Costs) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
The Medical Act 1983 (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order makes amendments to the Medical Act 1983 (“the Act”). Article 3 provides for a new statutory committee of the General Medical Council (“the Council”), to be called the Interim Orders Committee.

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) (Amendment) (England and Wales) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st August 2000, amend the Education (Student Loans) Regulations 1998 (“the principal Regulations”). The principal Regulations govern loans made under the Education (Student Loans) Act 1990, which are mortgage style repayment loans. Such loans are for the most part made to students who began their courses before 1st August 1998. Loans under the Teaching and Higher Education Act 1998, repayable through the tax system, are made to students beginning their courses after 1st August 1998.

items.title: The Motor Cars (Driving Instruction) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: This version of the The Motor Cars (Driving Instruction) (Amendment) Regulations 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The College of Care and Early Education (Dissolution) Order 2000
items.description: This Order dissolves with effect from 1st August 2000 the further education corporation established to conduct The College of Care and Early Education. It provides for the transfer of its property, rights and liabilities to City of Bristol College, and secures the rights of its employees by applying section 26(2) to (4) of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992.

items.title: The Tax Credits Schemes (Miscellaneous Amendments No. 3) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1973) (“the Family Credit Regulations”) and the Disability Working Allowance Regulations 1991 (S.I. 1991/2887) (“the Disability Working Allowance Regulations”).

items.title: The Charities (Bristol, Clifton and West of England Zoological Society) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to a Scheme sealed by the Charity Commissioners for England and Wales for the Charity known as the Bristol, Clifton and West of England Zoological Society (“the Charity”) which is regulated in part by the Bristol Clifton and West of England Zoological Society’s Act 1901 and the Bristol Clifton and West of England Zoological Society Act 1969 (“the Acts”).

items.title: The Safety of Sports Grounds (Designation) Order 2000
items.description: Article 2 of this Order designates the sports ground specified in the Schedule as a sports ground requiring a safety certificate under the Safety of Sports Grounds Act 1975.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Satellite Monitoring Measures) Order 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the enforcement of Articles 3 and 28c of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2847/93 establishing a control system applicable to the common fisheries policy and Commission Regulation (EC) 1489/97 laying down detailed rules for the application of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2847/93 as regards satellite-based vessel monitoring systems.

items.title: The Relevant Functions (Payments to Authorities) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the Relevant Functions (Payments to Authorities) Order 2000 (S.I. 2000/848) (“the principal Order”). It provides for the process and manner by which the Secretary of State may make payments to local authorities named in the second part of the Schedule to the principal Order in respect of expenses incurred in connection with the carrying out of relevant functions as defined in section 140EE of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (processing claims and conducting work-focused interviews).

items.title: The M621 Motorway (Speed Limit) Regulations 2000
items.description: This version of the The M621 Motorway (Speed Limit) Regulations 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Transfer of Functions (Agriculture and Fisheries) Order 2000
items.description: This Order transfers the roles of the Secretary of State for Scotland, the Secretary of State for Wales and the Secretary of State in the exercise of certain functions relating to agriculture and fisheries to the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food. Its purpose is to transfer functions which are in practice exercised by the Secretary of State for Scotland or the Secretary of State for Wales and which have become unnecessary following the devolution of functions to the Scottish Parliament and Executive and to the National Assembly for Wales.

items.title: The European Communities (Designation) (No. 2) Order 2000 (revoked)
items.description: This is a further Order in the series designating authorities which may exercise the power to make regulations conferred by section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972 and specifying the matters in relation to which that power may be exercised.

items.title: The Army, Air Force and Naval Discipline Acts (Continuation) Order 2000
items.description: Section 1(1) of the Armed Forces Act 1996 provides that the Army Act 1955, the Air Force Act 1955 and the Naval Discipline Act 1957 (“the Service Discipline Acts”) should continue in force until 31st August 1997 and should then expire unless continued in force in accordance with that section. The Army, Air Force and Naval Discipline Acts (Continuation) Orders 1997, 1998 and 1999 (S.I. 1997/1745, S.I. 1998/1499 and S.I. 1999/1734 respectively) have each provided for the continuation of the Service Discipline Acts for a period of 12 months. Under the 1999 Order, those Acts will continue in force until 31st August 2000. This Order enables the Service Discipline Acts to continue in force for a further period of 12 months beyond that date.

items.title: The International Seabed Authority (Immunities and Privileges) Order 2000
items.description: This Order confers privileges and immunities on the International Seabed Authority, its officials, representatives of its Members and experts performing missions for it, and on the Enterprise, an organ of the Authority. These privileges and immunities are conferred in accordance with the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (Cmnd. 8941) and the Protocol on the Privileges and Immunities of the International Seabed Authority (Cm. 4592). The Order will enable Her Majesty’s Government to give effect to that Convention and the Protocol and will come into force, with the exception of Article 15, on the date on which the Protocol enters into force in respect of the United Kingdom. Article 15 will come into force when the Enterprise operates independently of the Secretariat of the Authority.

items.title: The Pitcairn (Appeals to Privy Council) Order 2000
items.description: This Order regulates appeals from judgments of the Pitcairn Court of Appeal to Her Majesty in Council by prescribing the conditions under which such appeals may be brought and the procedure which must be followed.

items.title: The European Court of Human Rights (Immunities and Privileges) Order 2000
items.description: This Order confers privileges and immunities on the European Court of Human Rights, its judges, Registrar and Deputy Registrar, and on persons participating in proceedings before the Court or the Committee of Ministers of the Council of Europe. It will enable Her Majesty’s Government to give effect—

items.title: The Angola (United Nations Sanctions) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, amends the restrictions imposed by the Angola (United Nations Sanctions) Order 1993 pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in its resolution 864 of 15th September 1993, to restrict acts calculated to promote the supply or delivery of arms and related materiel, and petroleum and petroleum products, to any person in Angola.

items.title: The Angola (United Nations Sanctions) (Overseas Territories) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, amends the restrictions imposed by the Angola (United Nations Sanctions) (Dependent Territories) Order 1993 pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in its resolution 864 of 15th September 1993, to restrict acts calculated to promote the supply or delivery of arms and related materiel, and petroleum and petroleum products, to persons in Angola.

items.title: The Rail Vehicle Accessibility (LTS Rail Class 357 Vehicles) Exemption Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The Rail Vehicle Accessibility (LTS Rail Class 357 Vehicles) Exemption Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Eritrea and Ethiopia (United Nations Sanctions) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, amends the restrictions imposed by the Eritrea and Ethiopia (United Nations Sanctions) Order 2000 pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in its resolution 1298 of 17th May 2000, to restrict acts calculated to promote the supply or delivery of arms and related materiel to any person in Eritrea or Ethiopia. It also makes textual amendments to that Order.

items.title: The Eritrea and Ethiopia (United Nations Sanctions) (Overseas Territories) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, amends the restrictions imposed by the Eritrea and Ethiopia (United Nations Sanctions) (Overseas Territories) Order 2000, pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in its resolution 1298 of 17th May 2000, to restrict acts calculated to promote the supply or delivery of arms and related materiel to any person in Eritrea or Ethiopia. It also makes textual amendments to that Order.

items.title: The Sierra Leone (United Nations Sanctions) (Overseas Territories) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, applies to each of the territories specified in Schedule 1. It imposes restrictions, pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in its resolution 1306 of 5th July 2000, on the import of rough diamonds from Sierra Leone.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Republic of Korea) Order 2000
items.description: This Order makes provision for the modification of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 and the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 so as to give effect to the Convention on Social Security between the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Government of the Republic of Korea. The provisions relate to retirement pension.

items.title: The Civil Jurisdiction and Judgments Act 1982 (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: Part I of this Order amends the Civil Jurisdiction and Judgments Act 1982 in consequence of a revision of the 1968 Convention on Jurisdiction and the Enforcement of Judgments in Civil and Commercial Matters (“the Brussels Convention”) and the 1971 Protocol on the Interpretation of the Convention by the Court of Justice of the European Communities.

items.title: The Contracts (Applicable Law) Act 1990 (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the Contracts (Applicable Law) Act 1990. That Act gives effect, in the United Kingdom, to the 1980 Convention on the law applicable to contractual obligations (the Rome Convention) and the Luxembourg Convention of 1984 (by which Greece acceded to the Rome Convention). The Rome Convention harmonises the private international law rules of the Member States of the European Union on the law applicable to contracts.

items.title: The Exempt Charities Order 2000
items.description: This Order declares The Royal College of Art to be an exempt charity within the meaning of the Charities Act 1993 with effect from 1st August 2000. An exempt charity is not required to be registered with the Charity Commissioners and the Commissioners cannot exercise any powers under the Act in relation to an exempt charity except at the charity’s request.

items.title: The Attorney General’s Salary Order 2000
items.description: This Order increases the maximum salary payable under the Ministerial and Other Salaries Act 1975 to the Attorney General.

items.title: The Employment Relations (Offshore Employment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order in Council has the effect of applying certain provisions of the Trade Union and Labour Relations (Consolidation) Act 1992, and the Employment Rights Act 1996, relating to trade union recognition and industrial action, to employment for the purposes of activities in the territorial waters of the United Kingdom and specified areas of the Continental Shelf.

items.title: The National Assembly for Wales (Transfer of Functions) (Variation) Order 2000
items.description: The Order, which comes into force on 1st August 2000, is made under section 22 of the Government of Wales Act 1998. It varies the National Assembly for Wales (Transfer of Functions) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/672) by substituting in Schedule 1 to that Order a new entry for the Education Reform Act 1988. The effect of the variation is that the functions in that Act under section 218(6) and (6ZA) (so far as subsection (6ZA) relates to subsection (6)) (power to make regulations for prohibiting or restricting the employment of persons as teachers on medical or educational grounds, cases of misconduct etc) are, in relation to Wales, exercisable by both the National Assembly for Wales and the Secretary of State. Section 218(6ZA) is, prospectively, inserted by section 5(2) of the Protection of Children Act 1999.

items.title: The Data Protection Act 1998 (Commencement) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings the Data Protection Act 1998, with the exception of section 56 and those provisions already in force by virtue of section 75(2), into force on 1st March 2000. Section 75(4) of the Act provides that section 56 (prohibition of requirement as to production of certain records) must not be brought into force before sections 112, 113 and 115 of the Police Act 1997 (which provide for the issue by the Secretary of State of criminal conviction certificates, criminal record certificates and enhanced criminal record certificates) are all in force.

items.title: The National Assembly for Wales (Transfer of Functions) (No. 2) Order 2000
items.description: The Order, which comes into force on 2nd October 2000, is made under section 22 of the Government of Wales Act 1998. The effect of the Order is that where a court is considering making a declaration of incompatibility under section 4 of the Human Rights Act in relation to subordinate legislation made by the National Assembly for Wales, or made by a Minister of the Crown under a power that is also exercisable by the National Assembly, the National Assembly as well as the Minister of the Crown, will be entitled under section 5(2) of that Act of 1998, to be joined as a party to those court proceedings.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Modifications of Schedule 4) Order 2000
items.description: This Order makes certain modifications to Schedule 4 to the Scotland Act 1998 (“the 1998 Act”). That Schedule is concerned with the protection of enactments from modification by the Scottish Parliament.

items.title: The Angola (United Nations Sanctions) (Isle of Man) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, amends the restrictions imposed by the Angola (United Nations Sanctions) (Isle of Man) Order 1993. The restrictions are imposed pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in its resolution 864 of 15th September 1993. The amendment made by this Order restricts acts calculated to promote the supply or delivery of arms and related matériel or petroleum or petroleum products to any person in Angola.

items.title: The Angola (United Nations Sanctions) (Channel Islands) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, amends the restrictions imposed by the Angola (United Nations Sanctions) (Channel Islands) Order 1993. The restrictions are imposed pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in its resolution 864 of 15th September 1993. The amendment made by this Order restricts acts calculated to promote the supply or delivery of arms and related matériel or petroleum or petroleum products to any person in Angola.

items.title: The Eritrea and Ethiopia (United Nations Sanctions) (Isle of Man) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, amends the restrictions imposed by the Eritrea and Ethiopia (United Nations Sanctions) (Isle of Man) Order 2000, pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in its resolution 1298 of 17th May 2000, to restrict acts calculated to promote the supply or delivery of arms and related matériel to any person in Eritrea or Ethiopia.

items.title: The Eritrea and Ethiopia (United Nations Sanctions) (Channel Islands) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, amends the restrictions imposed by the Eritrea and Ethiopia (United Nations Sanctions) (Channel Islands) Order 2000 pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in its resolution 1298 of 17th May 2000, to restrict acts calculated to promote the supply or delivery of arms and related matériel to any person in Eritrea or Ethiopia.

items.title: The Data Protection (Corporate Finance Exemption) Order 2000 (revoked)
items.description: The Data Protection Act 1998 imposes certain obligations on data controllers to give data subjects information about the processing of personal data and to give access to personal data. By virtue of section 27(2) of that Act, the provisions imposing these obligations are referred to as “the subject information provisions”. Paragraph 6 of Schedule 7 to the Act creates an exemption from these provisions where, inter alia, the exemption is required for the purpose of safeguarding an important economic or financial interest of the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Sierra Leone (United Nations Sanctions) (Channel Islands) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, applies to the Channel Islands. It imposes restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in its resolution 1306 of 5th July 2000 on the import of rough diamonds from Sierra Leone.

items.title: The Medical Act 1983 (Medical Education) Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends section 4(2) of the Medical Act 1983 by adding a combination of Leicester University and Warwick University to the bodies and combinations of bodies entitled to hold qualifying examinations for the purposes of Part II of that Act.

items.title: The Water and Sewerage Undertakers (Inset Appointments) Regulations 2000
items.description: Section 7(4)(bb) of the Water Industry Act 1991 permits the appointment of a company seeking to replace the existing water or sewerage undertaker (the relevant undertaker) for particular premises where the two conditions prescribed in section 7(5) of the Act are satisfied. Section 7(5)(a) prescribes a threshold condition relating to the quantity of water supplied or likely to be supplied to the premises in any twelve month period.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (North-East Atlantic Control Measures) Order 2000
items.description: This Order makes provision for the enforcement of restrictions and obligations contained in Council Regulation (EC) No. 2791/99 (OJ No. L337, 30.12.99, p.1), for laying down certain control measures applicable in the area covered by the Convention on future multilateral cooperation in the North-East Atlantic fisheries (“the Council Regulation”). The Council Regulation in particular implements two recommendations of the North-East Atlantic Fisheries Commission establishing a scheme of control and enforcement applicable to fishing vessels in the Regulatory Area.

items.title: The London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect, subject to modifications, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the London borough of Hammersmith and Fulham. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The London Borough of Enfield (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the London borough of Enfield. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The London Borough of Brent (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the London borough of Brent. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The London Borough of Sutton (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the London borough of Sutton. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The Education (School Government) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (School Government) (England) Regulations 1999 (“the SGR”).

items.title: The Special Immigration Appeals Commission (Procedure) (Amendment) Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the Special Immigration Appeals Commission (Procedure) Rules 1998. The majority of the amendments are necessary as a result of, or are related to, the introduction of the “one-stop” procedure for appeals under Part IV of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999. These Rules also contain other minor amendments to the 1998 Rules.

items.title: The Data Protection (Conditions under Paragraph 3 of Part II of Schedule 1) Order 2000 (revoked)
items.description: Paragraph 2 of Part II of Schedule 1 to the Data Protection Act 1998 (“Part II”) provides that personal data will not be treated as processed fairly unless certain requirements are met relating to the provision to the data subject of information about the processing (“the information requirements”). Different provisions apply depending on whether data have been obtained from the data subject or from some other source.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (EPIRB Registration) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations require that EPIRBs (that is, emergency position indicating radio beacons) carried on United Kingdom ships and hovercraft shall be registered with the competent authority in a member State of the International Telecommunication Union (regulation 5(1)). In the United Kingdom the competent authority is HM Coastguard (regulation 4). (The registered particulars are maintained at the EPIRB Register, HM Coastguard, South Western, Pendennis Point, Castle Drive, Falmouth TR11 4WZ). A list of details required to be registered is included (regulation 5(2)), and changes must be notified (regulation 6). Penalties are provided for contravention of the Regulations (regulation 7).

items.title: The Human Rights Act 1998 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2000
items.description: This Order appoints 2nd October 2000 as the date on which those provisions of the Human Rights Act 1998 that are not already in force will come into force. The provisions give further effect to the European Convention on Human Rights.

items.title: The Data Protection (Functions of Designated Authority) Order 2000 (revoked)
items.description: Section 54(1) of the Data Protection Act 1998 provides that the Data Protection Commissioner (“the Commissioner”) shall continue to be the designated authority in the United Kingdom for the purposes of Article 13 of the Convention for the Protection of Individuals with regard to Automatic Processing of Personal Data which was opened for signature on 28th January 1981 (“the Convention”). Section 54(2) provides that the Secretary of State may by order make provision as to the functions to be discharged by the Commissioner in that capacity.

items.title: The Data Protection (Designated Codes of Practice) (No. 2) Order 2000 (revoked)
items.description: Article 2 of this Order designates for the purposes of section 32(3) of the Data Protection Act 1998 the codes of practice listed in the Schedule to this Order. Compliance with any of the designated codes may be taken into account when considering for the purposes of section 32(1)(b) of the Act whether the belief of a data controller that the publication of any journalistic, literary or artistic material would be in the public interest was reasonable.

items.title: The Data Protection (Miscellaneous Subject Access Exemptions) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the Data Protection (Miscellaneous Subject Access Exemptions) Order. That Order exempts from section 7 of the Data Protection Act 1998 (which entitles individuals to gain access to personal data held about them) personal data the disclosure of which is prohibited or restricted by certain enactments and subordinate instruments in the interests of safeguarding the interests of the data subject himself or the rights and freedoms of some other individual.

items.title: The Data Protection (Fees under section 19(7)) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations prescribe a fee of £2 to be paid to the Data Protection Commissioner for the supply of a duly certified copy of any data controller’s entry on the register.

items.title: The Welfare of Farmed Animals (England) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England only (regulation 1(2)), revoke and replace (with amendments):

items.title: The Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (UK Depositary Interests in Foreign Securities) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (UK Depositary Interests in Foreign Securities) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/2383) (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for UK depositary interests in foreign securities to be excluded from the definition of “chargeable securities” in Part IV of the Finance Act 1986 for the purposes of stamp duty reserve tax.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Extradition) (Amendment) Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates' Courts (Extradition) Rules 1989 (No. 1597) to take account of the coming into force of section 78 of, and Schedule 11 to, the Access to Justice Act 1999 (c. 22) which unify and rename the stipendiary bench.

items.title: The Youth Courts and Family Proceedings Courts (Constitution) (Amendment) Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the following Rules to take account of the coming into force of section 78 of, and Schedule 11 to, the Access to Justice Act 1999 (c. 22) which unify and rename the stipendiary bench:—

items.title: The Inner London Youth Courts (Selection of Chairmen) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the Inner London Youth Courts (Selection of Chairmen) Order 1990 to take account of the coming into force of section 78 of, and Schedule 11 to, the Access to Justice Act 1999 which unify and rename the stipendiary bench.

items.title: The Maintenance Orders (Facilities for Enforcement) (Amendment) Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the Maintenance Orders (Facilities for Enforcement) Rules 1922 (S.R. & O. 1922/1355) to take account of the coming into force of sections 74 and 75 of the Access to Justice Act 1999 (No. 22) which modify the territorial organisation of magistrates and magistrates' courts and section 78 of, and Schedule 11 to, that Act which unify and rename the stipendiary bench.

items.title: The Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (General) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations substitute a new version of regulation 48 of the Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (General) Regulations 1989, which sets out the relevant considerations and options available to courts when making or amending a legal aid order providing for the services of a Queen’s Counsel or for more than one advocate. They also amend regulation 54(1)(d), which provides that the Legal Aid Board can authorise the use of a Queen’s Counsel alone in cases where the legal aid order does not provide for this, by restricting this provision to magistrates' courts.

items.title: The Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (Costs) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (Costs) Regulations 1989 so that a Q.C. can agree to be instructed under a legal aid order providing for junior counsel and be remunerated at the rates applicable to junior counsel.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1993 (Extension of Group A Offences) Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends section 1(2) of the Criminal Justice Act 1993 by extending the Group A offences to which Part I of the Criminal Justice Act 1993 applies so as to include various offences contained in Part II of the Forgery and Counterfeiting Act 1981. Part I of the 1993 Act makes provision about the jurisdiction of courts in England and Wales in relation to certain offences of dishonesty and blackmail set out in section 1 of that Act.

items.title: The Gaming Clubs (Hours and Charges) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Gaming Clubs (Hours and Charges) Regulations 1984 in order to permit the extension of casino weekday opening hours from 4 a.m. until 6 a.m.

items.title: The Data Protection (Notification and Notification Fees) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations set out a number of arrangements in respect of the giving of notifications to the Data Protection Commissioner by data controllers under Part III of the Data Protection Act 1998.

items.title: The Income Tax (Sub-contractors in the Construction Industry) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Tax (Sub-contractors in the Construction Industry) Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/743, as amended by S.I. 1995/217, 448, 1996/981, 1998/2622, 1999/825, 2159 and 2000/1151). The effect of these Regulations is to add the Scottish Parliamentary Corporate Body to the persons treated as contractors for the purposes of Chapter IV of Part XIII of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (the Construction Industry Scheme), subject to fulfilling the minimum average annual expenditure levels provided by section 560(2A) and (2B) of that Act.

items.title: The General Medical Council (Legal Assessors) (Amendment) Rules 2000
items.description: The General Medical Council (Legal Assessors) Rules 1980 regulate the functions of legal assessors appointed by the General Medical Council to advise the Professional Conduct Committee, the Assessment Referral Committee, the Committee on Professional Performance, the Health Committee and the Preliminary Proceedings Committee on questions of law arising in proceedings before them.

items.title: The Watchet Harbour Revision Order 2000
items.description: This Order authorises West Somerset District Council in their capacity as the harbour authority for the harbour of Watchet to construct works at the harbour including an impounding wall for the construction and operation of a marina, the encasement of part of the harbour walls within the proposed marina, the construction of a sediment disposal pipe, the construction of a slipway alongside the Western Pier and dredging of various parts of the harbour.

items.title: Disability Rights Commission (Expenses) (Scotland) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations, which relate to Scotland only, provide for determination by the auditor of the sheriff court of the expenses incurred by the Disability Rights Commission in providing assistance under section 7 of the Disability Rights Commission Act 1999 in relation to proceedings which an individual brought or proposed to bring in the sheriff court.

items.title: The Data Protection Tribunal (Enforcement Appeals) Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules, which have been prepared after consultation with the Council on Tribunals regulate the exercise of the rights of appeal against decisions of the Data Protection Commissioner conferred by section 48 of the Data Protection Act 1998 and the practice and procedure of the Data Protection Tribunal in such cases.

items.title: The Data Protection (International Co-operation) Order 2000 (revoked)
items.description: Section 54(3) of the Data Protection Act 1998 provides that the Secretary of State may by Order make provision as to co-operation between the Data Protection Commissioner, the European Commission and other supervisory authorities in EEA States.

items.title: The Data Protection (Subject Access) (Fees and Miscellaneous Provisions) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations make miscellaneous provision in respect of the exercise of the right of access to personal data conferred in section 7 of the Data Protection Act 1998.

items.title: The Prescription Only Medicines (Human Use) Amendment Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the Prescription Only Medicines (Human Use) Order 1997 (the principal Order) by substituting a revised article 12, and inserting new articles 12A, 12B, 12C and Schedule 7. These amendments provide for the following new exemptions from the restrictions in section 58(2) of the Medicines Act 1968—

items.title: The Medicines (Sale or Supply) (Miscellaneous Provisions) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 1 to the Medicines (Sale or Supply) (Miscellaneous Provisions) Regulations 1980 by inserting a reference to additional classes of person to whom prescription only medicines and pharmacy medicines may be sold by way of wholesale dealing.
