items.description: This version of the The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) Red Route (Bus Priority) Experimental Traffic Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Feeding Stuffs Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England (save for regulations 11 and 12 and related provisions which apply to England, Wales and Northern Ireland) and come into force on 29th October 2000, revoke (regulation 26) and re-enact, or re-enact with amendments, provisions previously contained in the Feeding Stuffs Regulations 1995 as amended, implement the European Community Directives and Decision specified below, and provide for the enforcement of the European Community Regulations listed below.

items.title: The Health and Safety (Fees) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations update and replace the Health and Safety (Fees) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/645 as amended by S.I. 1999/2024, 1999/2597 and 1999/3232). They fix or determine the fees payable by an applicant to the Health and Safety Executive in respect of an application made for—an approval under mines and quarries legislation (regulation 2 and Schedule 1);an approval of certain respiratory protective equipment (regulation 3 and Schedule 2);an approval of plant or equipment under the Agriculture (Tractor Cabs) Regulations 1974 (regulation 4 and Schedule 3);an approval of a scheme or programme under the Freight Containers (Safety Convention) Regulations 1984 (regulation 5 and Schedule 4);a licence under the Asbestos (Licensing) Regulations 1983 (regulation 6 and Schedule 5);an approval of dosimetry services or type approval of apparatus under the Ionising Radiations Regulations 1999 (regulation 9 and Schedule 8);an approval, authorisation or licence etc. under the Explosives Act 1875 and certain instruments thereunder, for a licence under the Petroleum (Consolidation) Act 1928 or for the classification of an article, substance, combination or unit load under the Classification and Labelling of Explosives Regulations 1983 (regulation 10 and Schedule 9);an explosives licence under Part IX of the Dangerous Substances in Harbour Areas Regulations 1987 (regulation 12 and Schedule 10);an approval under the Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road (Driver Training) Regulations 1996 (regulation 14 and Schedule 12);an approval under the Health and Safety (First-Aid) Regulations 1981 (regulation 23 and Schedule 19);and in respect of—a vocational training certificate under the Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road (Driver Training) Regulations 1996 (regulation 13 and Schedule 11);a vocational training certificate under the Transport of Dangerous Goods (Safety Advisers) Regulations 1999 (regulation 15 and Schedule 13,);a notification under the Genetically Modified Organisms (Contained Use) Regulations 1992 (regulation 17 and Schedule 14);a notification or application under the Notification of New Substances Regulations 1993 (regulation 18 and Schedule 15).

items.title: The Community Drivers’ Hours (Passenger and Goods Vehicles) (Temporary Exception) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: Article 13(2) of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3820/85 of 20th December 1985 on the harmonisation of certain social legislation relating to road transport provides that Member States may in urgent cases grant a temporary exception for a period not exceeding 30 days to transport operations carried out in exceptional circumstances. These Regulations provide that until 12th October 2000 any time spent driving in the exceptional circumstances occasioned by the disruption of fuel supplies in Great Britain or the effects or consequences of such exceptional circumstances shall not be taken into account for the purposes of the application of Articles 6, 8(3), 8(4) or 8(5) of the Council Regulation.

items.title: The Motor Fuel (Designated Filling Stations and Fuel Depots) Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates certain filling stations and fuel depots in order that the Secretary of State may make a further order or orders or give directions under the Energy Act 1976 by reference to such designated premises. The List setting out the filling stations and depots concerned may be viewed on the world-wide web at www.dti.gov.uk.

items.title: The County of Cumbria (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect, without modification, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the county of Cumbria. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on or after 3rd May 2001.

items.title: The County of Cheshire (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect, without modification, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the county of Cheshire. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 3rd May 2001.

items.title: The County of Hertfordshire (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect, without modification, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the county of Hertfordshire. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 3rd May 2001.

items.title: The County of Lincolnshire (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect, subject to the modifications detailed below, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the county of Lincolnshire. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 3rd May 2001.

items.title: The County of Northamptonshire (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the county of Northamptonshire. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 3rd May 2001.

items.title: The County of Northumberland (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect, with modifications, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the county of Northumberland. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 3rd May 2001.

items.title: The County of Somerset (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect, with one modification, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the county of Somerset. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on or after 3rd May 2001.

items.title: The GLA Roads and Side Roads (Transfer of Property etc.) (Modification) (College Farm, Finchley) Order 2000
items.description: Article 2 of the GLA Roads and Side Roads (Transfer of Property etc.) Order 2000, among other matters, transferred to Transport for London certain property of the Secretary of State held for the purposes of his functions in relation to roads which on 3rd July 2000 became GLA roads. (i.e. roads for which Transport for London is the highway and traffic authority).

items.title: The Medicines (Sale or Supply) (Miscellaneous Provisions) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 2000
items.description: This version of the The Medicines (Sale or Supply) (Miscellaneous Provisions) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Transfer of Functions (Minister for the Civil Service and Treasury) Order 2000
items.description: This Order provides that functions with respect to the centralised procurement or provision of goods, services and facilities in the fields of computers and telecommunications which are exercisable by the Minister for the Civil Service may also be exercised by the Treasury. It transfers to the Treasury rights and liabilities in connection with those functions under contracts entered into before the commencement of the Order by the Minister for the Civil Service through the executive agency known as the Central Computer and Telecommunications Agency. The Order is consequential upon the transfer of the Central Computer and Telecommunications Agency from the Cabinet Office to the Office of Government Commerce in the Treasury.

items.title: The Judicial Committee (Osteopaths Rules) Order 2000
items.description: This Order contains the Rules governing procedure for all appeals to Her Majesty in Council under the Osteopaths Act 1993.

items.title: The Naval, Military and Air Forces Etc. (Disablement and Death) Service Pensions Amendment Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the Naval, Military and Air Forces Etc. (Disablement and Death) Service Pensions Order 1983 (“the principal Order”) which makes provision for pensions and other awards in respect of disablement or death due to service in the naval, military and air forces.

items.title: The Liquid and Gaseous Fuel (Designated Filling Stations and Fuel Depots) Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates certain filling stations and fuel depots in order that the Secretary of State may make a further order or orders or give directions under the Energy Act 1976 by reference to such premises. The list setting out the filling stations and depots concerned is an expanded version of the list referred to in article 2 of the Motor Fuel (Designated Filling Stations and Fuel Depots) Order 2000 (S.I. 2000/2484). That Order is revoked by this Order. The expanded list mentioned in article 2 of this Order may be viewed on the world-wide web at www.dti.gov.uk.

items.title: The Liquid and Gaseous Fuel (Designated Filling Stations and Fuel Depots) (No. 2) Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates certain filling stations and fuel depots in order that the Secretary of State may make a further order or orders or give directions under the Energy Act 1976 by reference to such premises. The list setting out the filling stations and depots concerned is an additional list which supplements that referred to in article 2 of the Liquid and Gaseous Fuel (Designated Filling Stations and Fuel Depots) Order 2000 (S.I. 2000/2522). The list mentioned in article 2 of this Order may be viewed on the world-wide web at www.dti.gov.uk.

items.title: The Animals and Animal Products (Import and Export) (England and Wales) (Amendment) (No. 1) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 3 to the Animals and Animal Products (Import and Export) (England and Wales) Regulations 2000 (S.I. 2000/1673 as amended) so as to implement the requirements of Commission Decision 2000/542/EC amending Decision 2000/528/EC concerning certain protection measures relating to Classical Swine Fever in the United Kingdom (OJ No. L231, 13.9.00, p.12).

items.title: The Medicines (Products Other Than Veterinary Drugs) (General Sale List) Amendment (No. 2) Order 2000
items.description: This Order further amends the Medicines (Products Other Than Veterinary Drugs) (General Sale List) Order 1984 (“the principal Order”) which specifies classes of medicinal products which can with reasonable safety be sold or supplied otherwise than by or under the supervision of a pharmacist (the general sale list).

items.title: The National Assembly for Wales (Transfer of Functions) Order 2000
items.description: Article 2(1) of, and Schedule 1 to, the Order provide (subject to the following provisions of article 2) for the exercise by the National Assembly for Wales (“the Assembly”) of statutory functions currently vested in Ministers of the Crown. All functions are vested in the Assembly for exercise in relation to Wales.

items.title: The Liquid and Gaseous Fuel (Designated Filling Stations and Fuel Depots) (No. 3) Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates certain filling stations and fuel depots in order that the Secretary of State may make a further order or orders or give directions under the Energy Act 1976 by reference to such premises. The list setting out the filling stations and depots concerned is an expanded version of the lists referred to in article 2 of the Liquid and Gaseous Fuel (Designated Filling Stations and Fuel Depots) Order (S.I. 2000/2522) and the Liquid and Gaseous Fuel (Designated Filling Stations and Fuel Depots) (No. 2) Order (S.I. 2000/2523). Those Orders are revoked by this Order. The expanded list mentioned in article 2 of this Order may be viewed on the world-wide web at www.dti.gov.uk.

items.title: The Building Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations are made under the Building Act 1984 (“the Act”). They revoke and replace with amendments the Building Regulations 1991 (“the 1991 Regulations”) and consolidate all subsequent amendments to those Regulations. They impose requirements on people carrying out certain building operations.

items.title: The Building (Approved Inspectors etc.) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations are made under the Building Act 1984 (“the Act”). They revoke and replace with amendments the Building (Approved Inspectors etc.) Regulations 1985 (“the 1985 Regulations”) and consolidate all subsequent amendments to those Regulations.

items.title: The Liquid and Gaseous Fuel (Designated Filling Stations and Fuel Depots) (No. 4) Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates certain further filling stations and fuel depots in order that the Secretary of State may make a further order or orders or give directions under the Energy Act 1976 by reference to such premises. The list setting out the filling stations and depots concerned supplements the list referred to in article 2 of the Liquid and Gaseous Fuel (Designated Filling Stations and Fuel Depots) (No. 3) Order 2000 (S.I. 2000/2530). The list mentioned in article 2 of this Order may be viewed on the world-wide web at www.dti.gov.uk.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (District of York) Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates the District of York, excluding that part of the A64 trunk road situated within the District, together with most of its on and off slip roads, and the A1237 road, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Liquid and Gaseous Fuel (Designated Filling Stations and Fuel Depots) (No. 5) Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates certain filling stations and fuel depots in order that the Secretary of State may make a further order or orders or give directions under the Energy Act 1976 by reference to such premises. The list setting out the filling stations and depots concerned is an expanded version of the lists referred to in article 2 of the Liquid and Gaseous Fuel (Designated Filling Stations and Fuel Depots) (No. 3) Order (S.I. 2000/2530) and the Liquid and Gaseous Fuel (Designated Filling Stations and Fuel Depots) (No. 4) Order (S.I. 2000/2533). Those Orders are revoked by this Order. The expanded list mentioned in article 2 of this Order may be viewed on the world-wide web at www.dti.gov.uk.

items.title: The Protection of Children (Access to Lists) (Prescribed Individuals) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations provide that, before the commencement of section 8 of the Protection of Children Act 1999 (1999 c. 14) and section 102 of the Care Standards Act 2000 (c. 14), a prospective adopter whose suitability to adopt a child is being considered under the Adoption Agencies Regulations 1983, (S.I. 1983/1964), is an individual of a prescribed description for the purposes of section 103(2)(b) of the Care Standards Act 2000. A person is therefore entitled, upon making an application to the Secretary of State, to information as to whether the prospective adopter is included either in the list kept under section 1 of the Protection of Children Act 1999, the list kept for the purposes of regulations made under section 218(6) of the Education Reform Act 1988 (c. 40), or any list kept of persons disqualified under section 470 or 471 of the Education Act 1996 (c. 56) for the purpose of considering his suitability to adopt.

items.title: The Nelson and West Merton Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the Nelson and West Merton Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the area for which the trust is established (article 3), its operational date (article 5) and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 6). It makes provision for the membership of the trust (article 4) and for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 7). It also contains a restriction on the exercise of functions by the trust (article 8).

items.title: The Regulation of Investigatory Powers Act 2000 (Commencement No. 1 and Transitional Provisions) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into force much of the Regulation of Investigatory Powers Act 2000.

items.title: The Care Standards Act 2000 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2000
items.description: This Order is the first commencement Order made under the Care Standards Act 2000 (“the Act”). Article 2(1) brings into force on 15th September 2000 section 99 of the Act, which makes provision relating to the inclusion of individuals named in past inquiries in the list kept by the Secretary of State for the purposes of section 1 of the Protection of Children Act 1999 (“the 1999 Act”) on transfer from the Consultancy Service Index; and section 96 in so far as it inserts a definition of “relevant inquiry” for those purposes. Article 2(2) brings into force on 2nd October 2000 sections 80(8) (for the purpose only of Regulations under section 103), 94, 96 (in so far as it is not already in force), 100, 101, 103, and 116 (in so far as it relates to paragraph 26 of Schedule 4, except paragraph (3)), and section 117(2) and Schedule 6 (repeals), in so far as they relate to sections 10 and 13(3) and (4) of the 1999 Act. Those provisions amend or replace provisions in the Protection of Children Act 1999, and make related provision. Article 3 makes transitional provision which is necessary in connection with the bringing into force of section 99 of the Act.

items.title: The Social Security Amendment (Capital Limitsand Earnings Disregards) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967), the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207), the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1971) and the Council Tax Benefit (General) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/1814).

items.title: The Road Traffic (Owner Liability) Regulations 2000
items.description: Schedule 1 to these Regulations prescribes forms for use in connection with sections 62 to 68 of and Schedule 4 to the Road Traffic Offenders Act 1988 (notices fixed to vehicles). Schedule 2 prescribes the particulars which must be contained in vehicle hiring agreements in order to attract the provisions of section 66 of the Act (hired vehicles).

items.title: The North Hampshire Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the North Hampshire Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the area for which the trust is established (article 3), its operational date (article 5) and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 6). It makes provision for the membership of the trust (article 4) and for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 7).

items.title: The Children (Protection at Work) (No. 2) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations give further effect, in England and Wales, to Council Directive 94/33/EC on the protection of young people at work (O.J. L216 of 20.8.94, page 12). Article 17(1)(b) provided a derogation to Article 8(1)(b), (maximum number of hours which may be worked by children in term time). This derogation has come to an end and these Regulations give full effect to this Article. Regulation 2 amends section 18 of the Children and Young Persons Act 1933, to place a limit on the number of hours that a child may be employed in any week in which they are required to attend shcool.

items.title: The Stamp Duty and Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (Definitions of Unit Trust Scheme) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Stamp Duty and Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (Definitions of Unit Trust Scheme) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/268) so as to provide that an employee share ownership plan approved under Schedule 8 to the Finance Act 2000 (c. 17) is not a unit trust scheme for the purposes of Part VII of the Finance Act 1946 (c. 64) or Part III of the Finance (No. 2) Act (Northern Ireland) 1946 (c. 17 (N.I.)) (unit trust schemes).

items.title: The Poole Bay Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the Poole Bay Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the area for which the trust is established (article 3), its operational date (article 5) and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 6). It makes provision for the membership of the trust (article 4) and for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 7). It also contains a restriction on the exercise of functions by the trust (article 8).

items.title: The Capital Gains Tax (Definition of Unit Trust Scheme) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Capital Gains Tax (Definition of Unit Trust Scheme) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/266) so as to provide that an employee share ownership plan approved under Schedule 8 to the Finance Act 2000 (c. 17) is not a unit trust scheme for the purposes of the Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992 (c. 12).

items.title: The Income Tax (Definition of Unit Trust Scheme) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Tax (Definition of Unit Trust Scheme) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/267) so as to provide that an employee share ownership plan approved under Schedule 8 to the Finance Act 2000 (c. 17) is not a unit trust scheme for the purposes of section 469 of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (c. 1) (tax treatment of unauthorised unit trusts).

items.title: The Local Government Pension Scheme (Management and Investment of Funds) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These regulations amend the Local Government Pension Scheme (Management and Investment of Funds) Regulations 1998 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Herefordshire Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the establishment order (“the Order”) of the Herefordshire Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), a Primary Care Trust established by Order under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977 (“the Act”) by removing the restriction in article 8 of the Order whereby, in the exercise of functions, the trust shall not provide services directly to patients, other than community health services.

items.title: The Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors (Training) Amendment Rules Approval Order 2000
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order, made by the United Kingdom Central Council for Nursing, Midwifery and Health Visiting, further amend the Rules approved by the Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors Rules Approval Order 1983 (S.I. 1983/873) to provide for the introduction of a new form, and a more flexible structure, of training to prepare student nurses for entry to Parts 12 to 15 of the register. The new provisions will apply to some courses which begin in September 2000 and will in due course replace the current training provisions. The Rules also amend the training provisions on health visitors to allow for more flexibility in the arranging of periods of attendance on a course and to add to the categories of nurse who may be admitted to training as a health visitor.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Life-Saving Appliances for Ships Other Than Ships of Classes III to VI(A)) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Merchant Shipping (Life-Saving Appliances for Ships Other Than Ships of Classes III to VI(A)) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/2721) (“the 1999 Regulations”). They have been made in consequence of defects in those Regulations, and are being issued free of charge to all known recipients of those Regulations.

items.title: The Learning and Skills Act (Commencement No. 2 and Savings) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings provisions of the Learning and Skills Act 2000 specified in Part I of the Schedule into force on 1st October 2000, the provisions of that Act specified in Part II of the Schedule into force on 1st November 2000, and the provisions of that Act specified in Part III of the Schedule into force on 1st January 2001. In one case the commencement is subject to savings.

items.title: The Poole Central and North Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the Poole Central and North Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the area for which the trust is established (article 3), its operational date (article 5) and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 6). It makes provision for the membership of the trust (article 4) and for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 7). It also contains a restriction on the exercise of functions by the trust (article 8).

items.title: The West London Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the West London Mental Health National Health Service Trust as an NHS trust, provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990.

items.title: The Regulation of Investigatory Powers (Notification of Authorisations etc.) Order 2000
items.description: This Order specifies the matters which must be notified to an ordinary Surveillance Commissioner when a person grants, renews or cancels a police or customs authorisation for the carrying out of intrusive surveillance under Part II of the Regulation of Investigatory Powers Act 2000.

items.title: The Merger Reference (Interbrew SA and Bass PLC) (Interim Provision) Order 2000
items.description: This Order requires Interbrew SA and Bass Holdings Limited (formerly a subsidiary of Bass PLC) to maintain the business of Interbrew SA separately from those of Bass Holdings Limited. The merger of Interbrew SA and enterprises formerly carried on by or under the control of Bass PLC has been referred to the Competition Commission. This Order, unless previously revoked, will cease to have effect 40 days after the Commission’s report is laid before Parliament, or on the failure of the Commission to report within the period allowed.

items.title: The Southampton East Healthcare Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the Southampton East Healthcare Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the area for which the trust is established (article 3), its operational date (article 5) and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 6). It makes provision for the membership of the trust (article 4) and for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 7). It also contains a restriction on the exercise of functions by the trust (article 8).

items.title: The Integrated Administration and Control System (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Integrated Administration and Control System Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/1317) (“the 1993 Regulations"), which make provision for the implementation in part of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3508/92 (OJ No. L355, 5.12.92, p.1) and Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 3887/92 (OJ No. L391, 31.12.92, p.36), as amended.

items.title: The South Manchester Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the South Manchester Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2000 by removing the restriction in article 8 concerning the provision of services directly to patients and by adding another electoral ward to the area for which the Primary Care Trust is established.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Value Added Tax Regulations S.I. 1995 No. 2518 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Great Central Railway (East Leake Branch, etc.) Order 2000
items.description: The Order authorises—

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance) (Rate of Discount for 2000/01) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: Part IV of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989 makes provision for the capital finance of local authorities. Section 49(2) sets out a formula for determining for the purposes of Part IV the value of the consideration falling to be given by a local authority under a credit arrangement in any financial year after the one in which the arrangement comes into being. The percentage rate of discount prescribed for the financial year is one of the factors referred to in the formula. For the financial year beginning on 1st April 2000 these Regulations prescribe 7.9 per cent. which is 0.5 per cent. less than the rate of discount prescribed for 1999/2000.

items.title: The District of Craven (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect, subject to one modification, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the district of Craven. With the exception of the changes made at article 4, the changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on or after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The Competition Act 1998 (Concurrency) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the Director General of Fair Trading and the regulators who can exercise functions of the Director under Part I the Competition Act 1998 concurrently with him to co-ordinate the performance of those functions.

items.title: The District of Hambleton (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the district of Hambleton. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 1st May 2003.

items.title: The Borough of Harrogate (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect, subject to the modification noted below, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the borough of Harrogate. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The District of Richmondshire (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the district of Richmondshire. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 1st May 2003.

items.title: The District of Ryedale (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the district of Ryedale. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 1st May 2003.

items.title: The Borough of Scarborough (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect, subject to one modification, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the borough of Scarborough. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 1st May 2003.

items.title: The District of Selby (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the district of Selby. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 1st May 2003.

items.title: The Isle of Wight (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Isle of Wight (Electoral Changes) Order 1999 (“the 1999 Order”) which will not now come into force. There are no substantive changes from the 1999 Order. The purpose of making the new Order is to secure the correction of boundaries of county divisions recorded incorrectly on the map referred to in the 1999 Order.

items.title: The Competition Commission Appeal Tribunal Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules prescribe the procedure to be followed before a tribunal constituted by the President of the Competition Commission Appeal Tribunals to hear an appeal made pursuant to either section 46 or 47 of the Competition Act 1998.

items.title: The GLA Roads (Continuity of Orders etc.) Order 2000
items.description: Article 3 of this Order provides for orders of specified types relating to non-motorway trunk roads in Greater London to have effect as if they referred to Transport for London in place of the Secretary of State and article 4 provides for the transfer of associated property, rights and liabilities. Article 5 puts Transport for London in the same position as the Secretary of State under the Town and Country Planning Act 1990 with regard to the carrying out of development in pursuance of such orders.

items.title: The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment No. 6) Order 2000
items.description: This Order mades changes to the Export of Goods (Control) Order 1994 (“the 1994 Order”) consequential on the making of the Dual-Use Items (Export Control) Regulations 2000 (S.I. 2000/), which revoke and replace the Dual-Use and Related Goods (Export Control) Regulations 1996 (“the 1996 Regulations”) on the day this Order comes into force.

items.title: Protection of Children Act Tribunal Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations make provision about the proceedings of the Tribunal established by section 9 of the Protection of Children Act 1999 on an appeal or determination under section 4 of that Act or regulations made under section 6 of that Act. They come into force on 2nd October 2000.

items.title: The Competition Act 1998 (Small Agreements and Conduct of Minor Significance) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations specify for the purposes of sections 39 and 40 of the Competition Act 1998 the category of agreements and conduct which enjoy limited immunity from the imposition of penalties for breach of the Chapter I or the Chapter II prohibitions respectively.

items.title: The Dual-Use Items (Export Control) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations consolidate the Dual-use and Related Goods (Export Control) Regulations 1996 and the various Regulations which amended them, and incorporate further amendments to give effect to certain provisions of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1334/2000 (“the new Council Regulation") on the control of exports of dual-use items and technology. The 1996 Regulations and the Regulations which amended them are revoked, but continue to have effect for any export which has occurred before 28th September 2000.

items.title: The Social Security Amendment (Enhanced Disability Premium) Regulations 2000
items.description: Regulation 2 of these Regulations amends the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967). A new premium (an “enhanced disability premium") is added to Schedule 2 (applicable amounts). Regulation 2 also makes minor consequential amendments to those Regulations.

items.title: The Competition Act 1998 (Notification of Excluded Agreements and Appealable Decisions) Regulations 2000
items.description: These regulations apply, with modifications, sections 13 to 16 of the Competition Act (“the Act”) (notification for guidance and decisions, effect of guidance and effect of a decision that the Chapter I prohibition has not been infringed) to cases where the Director has given a direction withdrawing an exclusion from the Chapter I prohibition, or is considering giving such a direction. They also apply those sections to applications in respect of agreements that benefit from a transitional period provided under Schedule 13 to the Act. The regulations apply, with modifications, the procedures under Schedule 5 in respect of applications under these regulations.

items.title: The Hartlepool Water (Amendment of Local Enactments) Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends references in local statutory provisions and charges schemes for the purposes of the replacement of Hartlepool Water Limited as water undertaker by Anglian Water Services Limited.

items.title: The North Surrey Water (Amendment of Local Enactments) Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends references in local statutory provisions and charges schemes for the purposes of the replacement of North Surrey Water Limited as water undertaker by Three Valleys Water plc.

items.title: The Prison (Amendment) (No. 2) Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the Prison Rules 1999.

items.title: The Young Offender Institution (Amendment) (No. 3) Rules 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Rules amend the Young Offender Institution Rules 1988.

items.title: The A66 Trunk Road (Sadberge Grade Separated Junction, Slip Road) Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The A66 Trunk Road (Sadberge Grade Separated Junction, Slip Road) Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Immigration (Regularisation Period for Overstayers) Regulations 2000
items.description: The effect of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999 (the Act) is to remove certain rights of appeal under the Immigration Acts which are currently enjoyed by those who overstay their leave.

items.title: The Community Drivers' Hours (Passenger and Goods Vehicles) (Temporary Exception) (Amendment) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Community Drivers' Hours (Passenger and Goods Vehicles) (Temporary Exception) Regulations 2000 (S.I. 2000/2483). They limit the exemption granted under those Regulations to drivers of tankers used for the carriage of petroleum products, and bring forward to 6th October 2000 the date on which the exemption will end for those drivers.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Deemed Supply of Goods) Order 2000
items.description: Paragraph 8 of Schedule 4 to the Value Added Tax Act 1994 provides that a supply of goods is deemed to be made in certain circumstances when a person ceases to be a taxable person. However, a supply is not deemed to be made if the VAT on the deemed supply would not be more than £250. The effect of this Order is that a supply will not be deemed to be made if the VAT on the deemed supply would not be more than £1,000.

items.title: The A30 Trunk Road (Honiton to Exeter Improvement) (Detrunking) (No. 2) Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The A30 Trunk Road (Honiton to Exeter Improvement) (Detrunking) (No. 2) Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A30 Trunk Road (Honiton to Exeter Improvement) (Trunking Part of A3015) Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The A30 Trunk Road (Honiton to Exeter Improvement) (Trunking Part of A3015) Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Milton Keynes Community Health National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the dissolution of the Milton Keynes Community Health National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The Aylesbury Vale Community Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the establishment order of the Aylesbury Vale Community Healthcare National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust established by Order under Part I of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990, by substituting a new article 3 (nature and functions of the trust).

items.title: The Investigatory Powers Tribunal Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules are for the Tribunal established under Part IV of the Regulation of Investigatory Powers Act 2000. The Tribunal has an extensive jurisdiction, set out in section 65(2) of the Act; these Rules only govern the jurisdiction described in paragraphs (a) and (b) of that subsection. The remainder of the subsection will be brought into force later, and there will be further rules for that purpose.

items.title: The Child Support, Pensions and Social Security Act 2000 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into force of sections 41 and 72 of the Child Support, Pensions and Social Security Act 2000 (c. 19).

items.title: The National Health Service (Functions of Health Authorities and Administration Arrangements) Amendment Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Functions of Health Authorities and Administration Arrangements) Regulations 1996 by making provision for Health Authorities to exercise the Secretary of State’s function of providing high security psychiatric services but only by making NHS contracts with Ashworth, Broadmoor or Rampton Hospital Authority or an NHS trust approved by the Secretary of State for the purpose of providing such services.

items.title: The Children (Allocation of Proceedings) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the Children (Allocation of Proceedings) Order 1991 so as to include Barnet County Court in the list of Family Hearing Centres.

items.title: The Commission Areas (Devon and Cornwall) Order 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the replacement of the two commission areas of Devon and Cornwall by a single commission area to be known as the Devon and Cornwall commission area, and comes into force on 1st January 2001.

items.title: The Specified Risk Material (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2000
The Finance Act 2000, section 5, (Appointed Day) Order 2000
items.description: This Order appoints 1st October 2000 as the day on which section 5 of the Finance Act 2000 (c. 17) (“the Finance Act”) comes into force.

items.title: The Liquid and Gaseous Fuel (Designated Filling Stations and Fuel Depots) (No. 5) (Revocation) Order 2000
items.description: This Order revokes the Liquid and Gaseous Fuel (Designated Filling Stations and Fuel Depots) (No. 5) Order 2000 (S.I. 2000/2535). That Order designated certain filling stations and fuel depots in order that the Secretary of State might make a further order or orders or give directions under the Energy Act 1976 by reference to such premises.

items.title: The Medicines (Exemptions for Merchants in Veterinary Drugs) (Amendment) Order 2000 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends articles 6 and 7 of the Medicines (Exemption for Merchants in Veterinary Drugs) Order 1998. It provides for gloves and laminated sheets with safety warnings to be supplied by agricultural merchants on any sale or supply of organophosphorus sheep dips. It takes away the right of marketing authorisation holders to sell veterinary medicines directly to users (though reserving their right to supply them free, subject to similar requirements in relation to such dips).

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Passenger Ships on Domestic Voyages) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 98/18/EC on Safety Rules and Standards for Passenger Ships (OJ L144, 15.5.1998, p. 1) (“the Directive”), which applies to the European Economic Area (EEA) by virtue of Joint Committee Decision 119/98 on environmental standards, maritime safety, maritime transport and safety.

items.title: The Railways (Safety Case) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-enact with modifications the Railways (Safety Case) Regulations 1994 which provided for railway operators to prepare safety cases and to submit them for acceptance to the infrastructure controller or to the Health and Safety Executive (“the Executive”).

items.title: The Lowestoft Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the Lowestoft Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the area for which the trust is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its operational date (article 5) and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 6). It makes provision for the membership of the trust (article 4) and for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 7).

items.title: The Social Fund Cold Weather Payments (General) Amendment Regulations 2000
The Pension Sharing (Consequential and Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Personal Pension Schemes (Disclosure of Information) Regulations 1987, the Occupational Pension Schemes (Winding Up) Regulations 1996, the Occupational Pension Schemes (Minimum Funding Requirement and Actuarial Valuations) Regulations 1996, the Occupational Pension Schemes (Disclosure of Information) Regulations 1996, the Register of Occupational and Personal Pension Schemes Regulations 1997 and the Occupational and Personal Pension Schemes (Levy) Regulations 1997 consequential upon the provision in Parts III and IV of the Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999 (c. 30) for the sharing of pensions following divorce or nullity of marriage (regulations 2 to 7).

items.title: The Personal Pension Schemes (Payments by Employers) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations make provision concerning the monitoring of employers' payments to personal pension schemes under section 111A of the Pension Schemes Act 1993 (c. 48).

items.title: The Sharing of State Scheme Rights (Provision of Information and Valuation) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations make provision in connection with the sharing, on divorce or nullity of marriage, of rights to the additional pension component of a state retirement pension.

items.title: The A249 Trunk Road (Iwade Bypass to Queenborough Improvement) (Detrunking) Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The A249 Trunk Road (Iwade Bypass to Queenborough Improvement) (Detrunking) Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A249 Trunk Road (Iwade Bypass to Neatscourt Roundabout and Bridge) Order 2000
The A249 Trunk Road (Iwade Bypass to Queenborough Improvement) (Slip Roads) Order 2000
The Immigration and Asylum Act 1999 (Commencement No. 7) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st January 2001 the following provisions of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999, so far as not already in force:

items.title: The Telecommunications (Lawful Business Practice) (Interception of Communications) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations authorise certain interceptions of telecommunication communications which would otherwise be prohibited by section 1 of the Regulation of Investigatory Powers Act 2000. To the extent that the interceptions are also prohibited by Article 5.1 of Directive 97/66/EC, the authorisation does not exceed that permitted by Articles 5.2 and 14.1 of the Directive.

items.title: The Education (Teachers' Qualifications and Health Standards) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Teachers' Qualifications and Health Standards) (England) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/2166) to enable certain teachers employed in independent schools to be awarded qualified teacher status.

items.title: The M65 Motorway (Junction 1A) (Speed Limit) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations impose a 50 miles per hour speed limit on a section of the westbound exit slip road of the M65 at Junction 1A, between a point 200.1 metres west of the centreline of the A49 Wigan Road and a point 440.7 metres west of the centreline of the A49 Wigan Road.

items.title: The Assured and Protected Tenancies (Lettings to Students) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 2 to the Assured and Protected Tenancies (Lettings to Students) Regulations 1998 to specify SOAS Homes Limited for the purpose of paragraph 8 of Schedule 1 to the Housing Act 1988. The effect of specification is that a tenancy granted by the body to a student at an educational institution specified in the 1998 Regulations is not an assured tenancy for the purposes of the 1988 Act.

items.title: The Essex County Council (Mayrose Bridge) Scheme 1999 Confirmation Instrument 2000
The Friendly Societies (Modification of the Corporation Tax Acts) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: Section 463(1) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“the Taxes Act”) provides that the Corporation Tax Acts shall apply to the life or endowment business carried on by friendly societies in the same way as they apply to mutual life assurance business or other long term business carried on by insurance companies, subject to such modifications and exceptions as may be prescribed by regulations made by the Treasury. The Friendly Societies (Modification of the Corporation Tax Acts) Regulations 1997 (“the principal Regulations”) make provision for such modifications and exceptions. These Regulations amend the principal Regulations with retrospective effect in the light of provisions of the Finance Act 2000 (c. 17) that amend or insert provisions in the Taxes Act.

items.title: The Partnerships (Unrestricted Size) No. 15 Regulations 2000
items.description: Section 716 of the Companies Act 1985 prohibits the formation of partnerships consisting of more than 20 persons. These Regulations exempt from that prohibition partnerships formed for the purpose of carrying on practice as actuaries, and consisting of persons the majority of whom are either Fellows of the Institute of Actuaries or of the Faculty of Actuaries, or full actuary members of one or more of the associations listed in the Schedule to the Regulations. They replace, and revoke, earlier Regulations (S.I. 1996/262) which provided for an exemption in relation to partnerships consisting of persons three-quarters or more of whom were Fellows of either the Institute of Actuaries or Faculty of Actuaries.

items.title: The Immigration (Designation of Travel Bans) Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates certain United Nations and European Union instruments for the purposes of section 8B of the Immigration Act 1971 (as inserted by section 8 of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999). Any person named by or under a designated instrument, or of a description specified in it, will as a result be excluded from the United Kingdom under section 8B(1), (2) or (3) (subject to the exceptions specified in article 3 of this Order).

items.title: The Regulation of Investigatory Powers (Source Records) Regulations 2000
items.description: Under section 29(2)(c) of the Regulation of Investigatory Powers Act 2000 a person may not grant an authorisation for the conduct or use of a covert human intelligence source unless he believes that arrangements exist that satisfy the requirements of section 29(5). For example, the arrangements must be adequate to ensure that the records relating to the source contain particulars of certain matters. Those matters are specified in these Regulations.

items.title: The Specified Risk Material (Amendment) (England) Order 2000
items.description: This Order (which gives effect to article 3.1 of Commission Decision 2000/418/EC regulating the use of material presenting risks as regards transmissible spongiform encephalopathies (OJ. No. L158, 30.6.2000, p.76)) amends the Specified Risk Material Order 1997 (S.I. 1997/2964) in so far as it extends to England. S.I. 1997/2964 extends to the whole of Great Britain.

items.title: The Electricity from Non-Fossil Fuel Sources Saving Arrangements Order 2000
items.description: This Order makes provision for the saving and modification of arrangements which have been made by public electricity suppliers in compliance with section 32 of the Electricity Act 1989 (“the Electricity Act”) to coincide with the commencement of trading under the new electricity trading arrangements (“NETA”). It also modifies section 32 and saves and modifies section 33 of the Electricity Act.

items.title: The Chippenham, Lackham and Trowbridge Colleges (Dissolution) Order 2000
items.description: This Order dissolves with effect from 1st November 2000 the further education corporations established to conduct Chippenham College, Lackham College, Wiltshire and Trowbridge College. It provides for the transfer of the property, rights and liabilities of each corporation to Wiltshire College, and secures the rights of its employees by applying section 26(2) to (4) of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992.

items.title: The Education Transfer Council (Winding up) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the dissolution of the Education Transfer Council by order of the Secretary of State (regulation 3) and for dealing with the property, rights and liabilities of the Council in connection with their dissolution (regulations 4 to 9) Regulation 4 deals with the preparation of a plan for the disposal of the property of the Council, regulations 5 and 6 deal with the exercise of the functions of the Council prior to the dissolution date and regulations 7 to 9 deal with property, rights and liabilities of the Council on dissolution, legal proceedings and construction of documents.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (EC Type Approval) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (EC Type Approval) Regulations 1998.

items.title: The Immigration Services Commissioner (Registration Fee) Order 2000
items.description: This Order prescribes the fee to be paid to the Immigration Services Commissioner on making an application for registration or continued registration with the Immigration Services Commissioner as required under Part V of Immigration and Asylum Act 1999 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Social Security (Contracting-out and Qualifying Earnings Factor and Revision of Relevant Pensions) Regulations 2000
items.description: The Regulations contained in this instrument are made before the end of the period of six months beginning with the coming into force of section 38 of the Child Support, Pensions and Social Security Act 2000 (c. 19) and are made in consequence of that Act.

items.title: The Distress for Rent (Amendment No. 2) Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules are consequential on the closure, by virtue of the Civil Courts (Amendment No. 2) Order 2000, of the county court at Caerphilly and remove references to it from the list of county courts in Appendix 3 to the Distress for Rent Rules 1988.

items.title: The Civil Courts (Amendment No. 2) Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the Civil Courts Order 1983 so as to close the county court at Caerphilly.

items.title: The Immigration Services Tribunal Rules 2000
items.description: This version of the The Immigration Services Tribunal Rules 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into force the Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998 for the purposes of the second phase, that is, in relation to commercial contracts for the supply of goods or services where the supplier is a small business and the purchaser is a small business (article 3). This is the fourth commencement order.

items.title: The East Gloucestershire National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the East Gloucestershire National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1990 (“the Establishment Order”) which established the East Gloucestershire National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The Income Tax (Sub-contractors in the Construction Industry and Employments) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Tax (Sub-contractors in the Construction Industry) Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/743) and the Income Tax (Employments) Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/744). The amendments made by regulations 2 and 3 substitute a new definition of “N” in the formulae to determine whether an employer is entitled to account, under those Regulations, to the Collector of Taxes quarterly for sums due in respect of income tax, National Insurance contributions, tax credits and student loan repayments. The new definition provides that, in addition to disregarding any adjustment to the amount payable to the collector in respect of the funding of tax credits by the employer, there shall also be disregarded any amount of secondary Class 1 contributions in respect of which liability has been transferred from the secondary contributor to the employed earner. Such a transfer of liability is possible under an election made jointly by the secondary contributor and the employed earner under paragraph 3B of Schedule 1 to the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 or the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Amendment No. 10) (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations (Northern Ireland 1979 (S.R. 1979 No 186) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Amendment No. 10) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (S.I. 1979/591) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The County Council of Norfolk (Construction of Old Barge Yard Foot/Cycle Bridge, Norwich) Scheme 2000 Confirmation Instrument 2000
items.description: This version of the The County Council of Norfolk (Construction of Old Barge Yard Foot/Cycle Bridge, Norwich) Scheme 2000 Confirmation Instrument 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The County Council of Norfolk (Construction of Cannon Wharf Foot/Cycle Bridge, Norwich) Scheme 2000 Confirmation Instrument 2000
items.description: This version of the The County Council of Norfolk (Construction of Cannon Wharf Foot/Cycle Bridge, Norwich) Scheme 2000 Confirmation Instrument 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Children’s Homes Amendment Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Children’s Homes Regulations 1991 in relation to England only, consequent on the commencement of section 40 of the Care Standards Act 2000 (2000 c. 14) also on 1st January 2001. Section 40 amends section 63(3)(a) of the Children Act 1989 so as to require privately operated children’s homes which accommodate and care for less than four children to be registered with the local authority in the same way as larger children’s homes. The effect of these Regulations is, subject to existing restrictions on fostering more than three children, to exempt homes in which children are accommodated as foster children from the requirement to register as a children’s home.

items.title: The Cosmetic Products (Safety) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Cosmetic Products (Safety) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/2925) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations 1999.

items.title: The Ecclesiastical Offices (Age Limit) (Channel Islands) Order 2000
items.description: This Order applies the Ecclesiastical Offices (Age Limit) Measure 1975 to the Channel Islands in accordance with a Scheme prepared by the Bishop of Winchester.

items.title: The Carriage by Air and Road Act 1979 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into force on 12th October 2000 those provisions of the Carriage by Air and Road Act 1979 specified in the Schedule to the extent there stated. This principally provides for section 4A to be inserted into the Carriage by Air (Supplementary Provisions) Act 1962. Section 4A provides for section 8A of the Carriage by Air Act 1961 to have effect in relation to a revision of the Convention set out in the Schedule to the 1962 Act as it has effect in relation to a revision of the Convention set out in Schedule 1 to the Act of 1961. This enables an Order in Council to be made to alter the Act of 1962 in consequence of any revision of the Convention set out in the Schedule to that Act.

items.title: The Landmines Act 1998 (Guernsey) Order 2000
items.description: This Order applies section 2(1) and (2) of the Landmines Act 1998 (prohibited conduct), as it has effect in the United Kingdom in respect of conduct outside the United Kingdom, to bodies incorporated under the law of the Bailiwick of Guernsey. It also extends that Act to the Bailiwick of Guernsey with the exceptions, adaptations and modifications specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Landmines Act 1998 (Isle of Man) Order 2000
items.description: This Order applies section 2(1) and (2) of the Landmines Act 1998 (prohibited conduct), as it has effect in the United Kingdom in respect of conduct outside the United Kingdom, to bodies incorporated under the law of the Isle of Man. It also extends that Act to the Isle of Man with the exceptions, adaptations and modifications specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Consumer Protection Act 1987 (Product Liability) (Modification) Order 2000
items.description: This Order in Council modifies Part I of the Consumer Protection Act 1987 (“the 1987 Act”). It implements Directive 1999/34/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 10th May 1999 (OJ No. L141, 4.6.99, p. 20). That Directive amends Council Directive 85/374/EEC (OJ No. L210, 7.8.85, p. 29) on the approximation of the laws, regulations and administrative provisions of the Member States concerning liability for defective products.

items.title: The Fixed Penalty Order 2000
items.description: This Order prescribes fixed penalties for fixed penalty offences. The prescribed penalties replace those prescribed for England and Wales by the Fixed Penalty Order 1992 (S.I. 1992/346) and for Scotland by the Fixed Penalty (Increase) (Scotland) Order 1992 (S.I. 1992/435 (S. 50)).

items.title: The Regulation of Investigatory Powers (Juveniles) Order 2000
items.description: Section 29 of the Regulation of Investigatory Powers Act 2000 allows authorisations to be granted for the use or conduct of covert human intelligence sources.

items.title: The Regulation of Investigatory Powers (Cancellation of Authorisations) Regulations 2000
items.description: Section 45 of the Regulation of Investigatory Powers Act 2000 imposes a duty on a number of persons to cancel authorisations under Part II of the Act, provided certain conditions are met.

items.title: The Care Standards Act 2000 (Commencement No. 1 (England) and Transitional Provisions) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the Care Standards Act 2000 (“the Act”) in relation to England only.

items.title: The Terrorism Act 2000 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into force on 12th October 2000 provisions of the Terrorism Act 2000 for the making of certain codes of practice and orders relating to such codes. The codes in question are those relating to the exercise of functions by members of the Royal Ulster Constabulary or the Armed Forces (in Northern Ireland), the exercise of functions by authorised and examining officers (within the meaning of the Act) and the audio or video recording of certain interviews by the police of suspected terrorists or as part of port or border controls.

items.title: The Education (Funding for Teacher Training) Designation (No. 3) Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates The Mathematical Association, a charitable unincorporated association having the registered charity number 313281, Plymouth City Council and Start Education (EM) Limited, a company limited by shares having the registration number 3244584 as institutions eligible for funding under Part I of the Education Act 1994.

items.title: The Gaming (Small Charges) Order 2000
items.description: This Order replaces the Orders revoked by article 3 of this Order. This Order increases from 50p to 60p the sum referred to in article 2(b).

items.title: The Gaming Act (Variation of Monetary Limits) (No. 2) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, which replaces the Orders revoked by article 3, increases the maximum monetary limits, specified in section 41 of the Gaming Act 1968, for the prizes, stakes and other matters mentioned in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Greater London Magistrates' Courts Authority (Financial Administration) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations apply Part VIII of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (financial administration) to the Greater London Magistrates' Courts Authority with modifications.

items.title: The Specified Risk Material (Amendment) (Wales) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 7th November 2000, gives effect to Article 3.1 of Commission Decision 2000/418/EC regulating the use of material presenting risks as regards transmissible spongiform encephalopathies (OJ No. L158, 30.6.2000, 76). It amends the Specified Risk Material Order 1997 (S.I. 1997/2964) in so far as it applies to Wales. The Specified Risk Material Order 1997 (“the 1997 Order”) applies to the whole of Great Britain.

items.title: The European Communities (Designation) ( No. 3) Order 2000 (revoked)
items.description: This is a further Order in the series designating authorities which may exercise the power to make regulations conferred by section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972 and specifying the matters in relation to which that power may be exercised.

items.title: The Harlow Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the Harlow Primary Care Trust (“the trust”). as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the area for which the trust is established (article 3 and the Schedule), its operational date (article 5) and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 6). It makes provision for the membership of the trust (article 4) and for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 7).

items.title: The Adoption (Intercountry Aspects) Act 1999 (Commencement No. 3) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into force on 16th October 2000 section 16(2) of the Adoption (Intercountry Aspects) Act 1999. This provision enables the Act to be deemed a pre-commencement enactment for the purpose of section 53 of the Scotland Act 1998. This will empower Scottish Ministers to exercise the functions contained in the Act for Scotland.

items.title: The Judicial Committee (Chiropractors Rules) Order 2000
items.description: This Order contains the Rules governing procedure for appeals to Her Majesty in Council under the Chiropractors Act 1994.

items.title: The Amalgamation of the Alford, Louth and Skegness District Internal Drainage Districts Order 2000
items.description: This Order confirms a Scheme submitted by the Environment Agency for the abolition of the Alford, Louth and Skegness District Internal Drainage Boards.

items.title: The Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (General) (Amendment) (No. 5) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 48 of the Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (General) Regulations 1989 to permit the Registrar of Criminal Appeals to make or amend a legal aid order so as to provide for the services of a Queen’s Counsel or of more than one advocate in the Court of Appeal.

items.title: The Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 2000 (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 7th November 2000, amend the Mandatory Awards Regulations 2000. Paragraph 14 of Schedule 2 to the Mandatory Awards Regulations 2000 is amended so that grant may be paid in respect of a dependent child who attends a city academy (regulation 3). Paragraph 1(1)(c) of Schedule 3 to the Mandatory Awards Regulations 2000 is amended to extend the disregard of grants to students to facilitate teacher training, in calculating their income, to payments in respect of such training by institutions which receive funding under section 65(3) of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992 (regulation 4).

items.title: The Local Government Pension Scheme (Merseyside Transport Limited) Regulations 2000
items.description: The Regulations provide that employees of Merseyside Transport Limited who are members of, or eligible to be members of, the Local Government Pension Scheme (“the Scheme”), constituted by the Local Government Pension Scheme Regulations 1997 (“the principal Regulations”) may remain in the Scheme or become members of the Scheme, after the transfer of their employment to MTL (Gilmoss) Limited on the transfer by Merseyside Transport Limited of its Gilmoss bus depot business.

items.title: The North Peterborough Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the North Peterborough North Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the area for which the trust is established (article 3), its operational date (article 5) and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 6). It makes provision for the membership of the trust (article 4) and for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 7). It also contains a restriction on the exercise of functions by the trust (article 8).

items.title: The Genetically Modified Organisms(Contained Use) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations have effect with a view to protecting persons and the environment from risks arising from activities involving the contained use of genetically modified micro-organisms and protecting persons from risks arising from activities involving the contained use of genetically modified organisms which are not micro-organisms.

items.title: The Education (School Performance Information) (England) (Amendment No. 3)Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Education (School Performance Information) (England) Regulations 1999 (“the principal Regulations”) which relate to the collection and publication of information about the performance of schools.

items.title: The Local Government Act 2000 (Commencement No. 3) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into force, in relation to England only, further provisions of the Local Government Act 2000 (“the Act”).

items.title: The South Peterborough Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the South Peterborough Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the area for which the trust is established (article 3), its operational date (article 5) and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 6). It makes provision for the membership of the trust (article 4) and for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 7). It also contains a restriction on the exercise of functions by the trust (article 8).

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) (No. 3) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, safety zones having a radius of 500 metres from the specified point, around each installation specified in the Schedule to this Order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29)) (article 2).

items.title: The Cribbs Causeway—Easton-in-Gordano Special Roads Scheme 1964 (Variation) Scheme 2000
items.description: This Scheme varies the Cribbs Causeway—Easton-in-Gordano Special Roads Scheme 1964 by removing the existing footway over the Avonmouth Bridge and providing for the existing cycle track to become a combined cycle track and footway.

items.title: The Local Government Act 2000 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into force, in relation to England only, further provisions of the Local Government Act 2000 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Tendring Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the Tendring Primary Care Trust (“the trust”), as provided for under section 16A of the National Health Service Act 1977. It specifies the area for which the trust is established (article 3), its operational date (article 5) and limitations on the exercise of its functions during its preparatory period (article 6). It makes provision for the membership of the trust (article 4) and for assistance to the trust during its preparatory period (article 7). It also contains a restriction on the exercise of functions by the trust (article 8).

items.title: The Local Authorities (Proposals for Alternative Arrangements) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: Under Part II of the Local Government Act 2000 (“the Act”), local authorities are required to make arrangements for the discharge of their functions by executives which must take one of the forms specified in section 11(2) to (4) of the Act or in regulations under section 11(5). Section 25 of the Act requires local authorities to draw up proposals for executive arrangements. Section 31 of the Act permits a local authority to which that section applies to draw up proposals for alternative arrangements of a particular type permitted by regulations under section 32 of the Act. These regulations contain requirements as to those proposals for alternative arrangements.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Arrangements for the Discharge of Functions) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: Part II of the Local Government Act 2000 enables a local authority to make arrangements for the creation and operation of an executive which is responsible for certain functions of the local authority. The executive must take one of the forms specified in or under section 11 of that Act.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Referendums) (Petitions and Directions) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: Under Part II of the Local Government Act 2000 (“the Act”), local authorities are able to make arrangements for the discharge of their functions by executives, which must take one of the forms specified in section 11(2) to (4) of the Act or in regulations under section 11(5). Where an authority’s proposals for an executive involve an elected mayor, they are required by section 27 of the Act to hold a referendum before taking steps to implement the proposals.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Functions and Responsibilities) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: Part II of the Local Government Act 2000 provides for the discharge of a local authority’s functions by an executive of the authority (which must take one of the forms specified in section 11(2) to (5) of the Act), unless those functions are specified as functions that are not to be the responsibility of the authority’s executive.

items.title: The Social Security Commissioners (Procedure) (Amendment) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security Commissioners (Procedure) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/1495). The amendments are in consequence of the transfer under the Tax Credits Act 1999 of functions relating to family credit and disability working allowance (now known as working families' tax credit and disabled person’s tax credit) from the Department of Social Security to the Treasury and the Board of Inland Revenue.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation(i-21 Ltd) Order 2000
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to i-21 Ltd a licence on 20th April 2000 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 25th October 2000. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, i-21 Ltd will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 23rd November 2000.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Pacific Gateway Exchange (UK) Limited) Order 2000
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Pacific Gateway Exchange (UK) Limited a licence on 20th April 2000 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in the licence.
