Section 9(2)(c) of the 1971 Act provides that references to the “rate” of pension shall have effect as references also to the amount of a lump sum benefit. The increase is the percentage (or in some circumstances a fraction o f the percentage) by which the Secretary of State for Social Security has, by direction given under the provisions of section 151(1) of the Social Security Administration Act 1992, increased the sums referred to in section 150(1)(c) of that Act. These are the sums which are the additional pensions in long term benefits, namely the additional pensi on entitlements accruing to employees in respect of earnings after 5th April 1978.

items.title: The Industrial and Provident Societies (Fees) Regulations 2000
items.description: These regulations increase the fees payable for registration and other transactions under the Industrial and Provident Societies Acts 1965 and 1967. They revoke the Industrial and Provident Societies (Fees) Regulations 1999. Taking all the fees together, some of which remain unchanged, the overall increase is about 3½%. The fee changes are as follows:–

items.title: The Friendly Societies (General Charge and Fees) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations provide for a general charge to be paid by friendly societies towards the expenses of the Friendly Societies Commission. The charge is levied with respect to the Commission’s accounting year beginning 1st April 2000 and is expected to raise £1.6 million (an increase of 26% from that raised for the accounting year beginning 1st April 1999). In accordance with Schedule 1, each society is required to pay 0.55% of its “specified income” for the year to 31st December 1999, subject to a minimum charge of £350 and a maximum of £47,000. (Under the Friendly Societies (General Charge and Fees) Regulations 1999 (“the 1999 Regulations”) the percentage was 0.435%, and the minimum and maximum £300 and £36,000 respectively.

items.title: The Petty Sessions Areas (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order substitutes a new list for the list of petty sessions areas in the Petty Sessions Areas Order 1999. The new list substituted by this Order takes account of changes to magistrates' courts committee areas that come into force on 1st April 2000.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts Committee Areas (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order substitutes a new list for the list of magistrates' courts committee areas in the Magistrates' Courts Committee Areas Order 1999. The list in that Order was the list of magistrates' courts committee areas in existence immediately before 27th September 1999. The new list substituted by this Order takes account of changes that were effected by Orders made before 27th September 1999, but that come into force on 1st April 2000.

items.title: The Justices of the Peace (Commission Areas) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order substitutes a new list for the list of commission areas in the Justices of the Peace (Commission Areas) Order 1999. The list in that Order was the list of commission areas in existence immediately before 27th September 1999. The new list substituted by this Order takes account of changes to magistrates' courts committee areas that were affected by Orders made before 27th September 1999, but that come into force on 1st April 2000.

items.title: The Social Security (Approved Work) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967), the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207), the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1971), the Council Tax Benefit (General) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/1814), the Social Security (New Deal Pilot) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/3156), the Social Security (Incapacity for Work) (General) Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/311), the Social Security (Incapacity Benefit) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/2946), the Social Security (Incapacity Benefit—Increases for Dependants) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/2945), the Social Security (Severe Disablement Allowance) Regulations 1984 (S.I. 1984/1303) and the Social Security Benefit (Computation of Earnings) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/2745).

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend two sets of regulations related to the Pensions Act 1995 pursuant to section 10 of the Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999, replacing section 49(8) of the 1995 Act with new subsections (8) to (13). Like the earlier section 49(8), these subsections are concerned with the regulation of payments by employers to occupational pension schemes. However, whereas previously an employer was liable to a criminal penalty if he breached the requirement that deductions from an employee’s earnings in respect of contributions to an occupational pension scheme be paid to the trustees or managers within a prescribed period, the substituted provisions give the Occupational Pensions Regulatory Authority (OPRA) the power to impose a civil sanction for breach of that requirement. A person found guilty of the fraudulent evasion of the obligation to make timely payment, will though, remain liable to a criminal penalty.

items.title: The Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967), the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/207), the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1971) and the Council Tax Benefit (General) Regulations 1992 (S.I. 1992/1814) (“the principal sets of regulations").

items.title: The Disabled Persons (Badges for Motor Vehicles) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations replace, in relation to England the Disabled Persons (Badges for Motor Vehicles) Regulations 1982. They consolidate those Regulations with modifications in their application to England.

items.title: The Local Authorities' Traffic Orders (Exemptions for Disabled Persons) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations replace the Local Authorities' Traffic Orders (Exemptions for Disabled Persons) (England and Wales) Regulations 1986, in relation to England.

items.title: The Carriers' Liability (Clandestine Entrants) (Code of Practice) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 3rd April 2000 the Code of Practice, issued pursuant to section 33 of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999, to be followed by any person operating a system for preventing the carriage of clandestine entrants. As sections 32 and 34 to 37 of that Act, which provide for a penalty for the carriage of clandestine entrants, are commenced on 3rd April 2000 by S.I. 2000/464 (C. 12) for the purposes only of clandestine entrants concealed in vehicles, the code of practice is also confined to vehicles. The Code of Practice is taken into account in determining whether a person who has transported clandestines was operating an effective system for preventing their carriage; and thus whether he has a defence to the penalty.

items.title: The Carriers' Liability (Clandestine Entrants and Sale of Transporters) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations concern the penalty for carrying clandestine entrants established by, and the sale of transporters under, Part II of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999.

items.title: The Transfer of Undertakings (Protection of Employment) (Greater London Authority) Order 2000
items.description: This Order applies the Transfer of Undertakings (Protection of Employment) Regulations 1981 to transfers of staff by means of transfer orders under section 408 or transfer schemes under section 409 of the Greater London Authority Act 1999. Those Regulations are applied only if they do not otherwise apply to such transfers.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Designation of Receipts) Order 2000
items.description: This Order specifies the receipts of the Scottish Ministers and other office-holders in the Scottish Administration which are to be designated receipts. Under the Scotland Act 1998 the Scottish Ministers must pay to the Secretary of State sums equal to the amount of designated receipts.

items.title: The Social Security (Maternity Allowance) (Earnings) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations provide for the payments which are, or are to be treated as, earnings for the purpose of determining entitlement to State Maternity Allowance.

items.title: The Measuring Instruments (EEC Requirements) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Measuring Instruments (EEC Requirements) (Fees) Regulations 1998 (S.I. 1998/1177) (the principal Regulations) to provide for an increase in the fees in respect of services provided by the Department of Trade and Industry in relation to the type approval and testing of measuring instruments. The hourly rate (as specified in paragraph 1(a) respectively in Schedules 2, 3, 5 and 6 to the principal Regulations) for these services is increased from £65 to £70 (an increase of £5) and is the first increase since 1st June 1998 (when the principal Regulations came into force).

items.title: The Social Security (Maternity Allowance) (Work Abroad) (Amendment) (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Maternity Allowance) (Work Abroad) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (S.R. 1987 No. 151) (“the principal Regulations”). The amendments are in consequence of changes made by Article 50 of the Welfare Reform and Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/3147 (N.I. 11)) to the conditions for a maternity allowance under section 35 of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (c. 7). The purpose of the amendments to the principal Regulations is to ensure that they continue to enable women who are ordinarily resident in Northern Ireland but who have worked abroad to satisfy, in specified circumstances, certain of the conditions for a maternity allowance.

items.title: The Social Security (Maternity Allowance) (Work Abroad) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Maternity Allowance) (Work Abroad) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/417) (“the principal Regulations”). The amendments are in consequence of changes made by section 53 of the Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999 (c. 30) to the conditions for a maternity allowance under section 35 of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 (c. 4). The purpose of the amendments to the principal Regulations is to ensure that they continue to enable women who are ordinarily resident in Great Britain but who have worked abroad to satisfy, in specified circumstances, certain of the conditions for a maternity allowance.

items.title: The Conditional Fee Agreements Regulations 2000
items.description: Section 58(1) of the Courts and Legal Services Act 1990 provides that a conditional fee agreement is not unenforceable if it satisfies certain conditions. These include conditions to be specified in regulations under section 58(3) of that Act. Regulations 2 and 3 specify those conditions. Regulation 2 applies to all conditional fee agreements. Regulation 3 sets out further requirements applying only to agreements which provide for success fees.

items.title: The Access to Justice (Membership Organisations) Regulations 2000
items.description: Section 30 of the Access to Justice Act 1999 applies where a body of a description to be specified in regulations undertakes (in accordance with arrangements satisfying conditions to be so specified) to meet liabilities which members of the body or other persons who are parties to proceedings may incur to pay the costs of other parties. Regulation 2 of these Regulations specifies bodies which are for the time being approved by the Lord Chancellor for this purpose. Regulation 3 specifies the conditions which the arrangements must satisfy.

items.title: The Health Act 1999 (Supplementary, Consequential etc. Provisions) (No. 2) Order 2000
items.description: This Order makes amendments to certain enactments consequential upon provisions of the Health Act 1999 (“the Act”) concerning Primary Care Trusts and high security psychiatric services.

items.title: The Primary Care Trusts (Functions) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations make provision for functions of Health Authorities to be exercised by Primary Care Trusts and for certain restrictions on the exercise of those functions by Primary Care Trusts. The Regulations apply to Health Authorities and Primary Care Trusts in England.

items.title: The Health Authorities (Membership and Procedure) Amendment Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to regulations concerning the membership and procedure of Health Authorities and Special Health Authorities. The amendments apply to Health Authorities in England and to Special Health Authorities.

items.title: The Workmen’s Compensation (Supplementation) (Amendment) Scheme 2000
items.description: This Scheme amends the Workmen’s Compensation (Supplementation) Scheme 1982 by making adjustments to the rate of lesser incapacity allowance consequential upon the increase in the maximum rate of that allowance made by the Social Security Benefits Up-rating Order 2000. The Scheme also makes transitional provision.

items.title: The Dairy Produce Quotas (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st April 2000, amend, in so far as they apply to England, the Dairy Produce Quotas Regulations 1997 (S.I. 1997/733).

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (City of Bristol) Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates the County of the City of Bristol, excluding those parts of the M5, M32 and M49 Motorways situated within the County, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Retailers’ Records for Veterinary Medicinal Products Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations complete the implementation of Article 50b.2 and .3 of Council Directive 81/851/EEC (OJ No. L317, 6.11.81. p. 1) on the approximation of the laws of the Member States relating to veterinary medicinal products as amended by Council Directive 90/676/EEC (OJ No. L373, 31.12.90, p. 15). Previous implementing provisions are in S.I. 1998/1044.

items.title: The Young Offender Institution (Amendment) Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules amend the Young Offender Institution Rules 1988 (“the 1988 Rules”). Rule 2 of these Rules introduces the Schedule, which sets out the amendments to the 1988 Rules.

items.title: The Homelessness (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations prescribe classes of persons subject to immigration control who are eligible for housing assistance under Part VII of the Housing Act 1996 (homelessness) and describe other persons who are not eligible. They also prescribe periods for notices and applications for the purposes of Part VII. These Regulations extend to England only. They revoke and re-enact with changes the Homelessness Regulations 1996 in so far as they extend to England.

items.title: The Allocation of Housing (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-enact with changes the Allocation of Housing Regulations 1996 in so far as they extend to England. These Regulations extend to England only.

items.title: The Education Standards Fund (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations supersede the Education (Education Standards Etc. Grants) (England) Regulations 1999, as amended, which are revoked.

items.title: The Asylum Support Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations make provision supplementing Part VI of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999. They have the effect that support is to be available to asylum-seekers and their dependants who apply in accordance with the Regulations and appear to the Secretary of State to be destitute, or to be likely to become destitute within 14 days of the application being considered. The Regulations define who is a “dependant” of an asylum-seeker for these purposes and set out the matters to be taken into account in deciding whether a person, or family group, is destitute. They also set out what support can be expected to be provided to a successful applicant: this will generally take the form of accommodation, provision for other essential living needs or both accommodation and such provision. The provision for essential living needs will be in the form of vouchers redeemable for goods, services and not more than £10 cash per person per week.

items.title: The Immigration (Eligibility for Assistance) (Scotland and Northern Ireland) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations provide that, for specified purposes, section 115 of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999 (exclusion from benefit) does not apply in Scotland or Northern Ireland on and after 3rd April 2000 to certain people who, until that date, were not eligible for certain social security benefits but were eligible for assistance under the Social Work (Scotland) Act 1968, the Mental Health (Scotland) Act 1984 or the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972. The effect of these Regulations is to preserve that eligibility on and after that date.

items.title: The Persons subject to Immigration Control (Housing Authority Accommodation and Homelessness) Order 2000
items.description: This Order revokes, in so far as they extend to England, Scotland or Northern Ireland, all extant Orders made under section 9 of the Asylum and Immigration Act 1996 (article 10). Sections 118 and 119 of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999 replace section 9 of the 1996 Act. This Order specifies classes of persons subject to immigration control for the purposes of section 118 (articles 3 to 6) and section 119 (articles 7 to 9).

items.title: The Airports (Designation) (Removal and Disposal of Vehicles) (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: Section 66 of the Airports Act 1986 empowers the Secretary of State to direct that, in their application to land within an airport designated by him, certain provisions of the Refuse Disposal (Amenity) Act 1978 and regulations in force thereunder shall have effect subject to such modifications as appear to him to be necessary or expedient to confer on the airport operator the functions exercisable under those provisions by local authorities.

items.title: The Council Tax (Liability of Owners) (Amendment) (Scotland) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations prescribe a new class of dwellings for which the owner, as opposed to the resident, is responsible for paying council tax, in the Council Tax (Liability of Owners) (Scotland) Regulations 1992.

items.title: The Relocation Grants (Form of Application) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Form set out in the Schedule to the Relocation Grants (Form of Application) Regulations 1997, to be used for an application for relocation grant payable under sections 131 to 140 of the Housing Grants, Construction and Regeneration Act 1996.

items.title: The Employment Zones Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations designate 15 areas in Great Britain to be employment zones within which employment zone programmes can be run. The purpose of such a programme is to assist participants living within a zone and claiming a jobseeker’s allowance to obtain sustainable employment. Employment is defined widely to include any paid work whether carried out for an employer or in the course of any trade, business, profession, partnership, office or vocation.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (County of Kent) (District of Shepway) Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates the District of Shepway, excluding the M20 Motorway and the A20 trunk road, together with their on and off slip roads, for the whole of their lengths in the District, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (S.I. 1979/591) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security Amendment (Employment Zones) Regulations 2000
items.description: The Regulations contained in this Instrument are made in consequence of provisions in section 60 of the Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999 (c. 30). The instrument is made before the end of the period of six months beginning with the coming into force of those provisions; the regulations in it are therefore exempted from the requirement in section 172(1) of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (c. 5) to refer proposals to make these Regulations to the Social Security Advisory Committee and are made without reference to that Committee.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Compulsory Insurance) Regulations 2000
items.description: Council Directive 72/166/EEC (OJ No. L103, 2.5.72, p. 2), as modified by Council Directives 84/5/EEC (OJ No. L8, 11.1.84, p. 17) and 90/232/EEC (OJ No. L129, 19.5.90, p. 33) requires a Member State to take all appropriate measures to ensure that civil liability in respect of the use of motor vehicles normally based in its territory is covered by insurance.

items.title: The Social Security Contributions (Intermediaries) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 6th April 2000, make provision for ensuring that social security contributions payable in relation to employed earner’s employment remain payable notwithstanding the existence of arrangements whereby the services of the worker for another person (“the client”) are performed through another person (“the intermediary”) and not pursuant to a contract of employment between the worker and the client.

items.title: The Social Security Contributions (Intermediaries) (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 6th April 2000, make provision for ensuring that social security contributions payable in relation to employed earner’s employment remain payable notwithstanding the existence of arrangements whereby the services of the worker for another person (“the client”) are performed through another person (“the intermediary”) and not pursuant to a contract of employment between the worker and the client.

items.title: The Social Fund Winter Fuel Payment Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: This version of the The Social Fund Winter Fuel Payment Regulations 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Radio Equipment and Telecommunications Terminal Equipment Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations implement Directive 1999/5/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on radio equipment and telecommunications terminal equipment and the mutual recognition of their conformity.

items.title: The Data Protection Tribunal (National Security Appeals) (Telecommunications) Rules 2000
items.description: These Rules regulate the exercise of the rights of appeal conferred by regulation 32 of the Telecommunications (Data Protection and Privacy) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/2093) (relating to Ministerial certification that exemption from provisions of the Regulations is or was required for the purpose of safeguarding national security), and the practice and procedure of the Data Protection Tribunal in such cases.

items.title: The West Hertfordshire Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the West Hertfordshire Hospitals National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust (article 3) and the number of executive and non-executive directors (article 4). It specifies the operational date (the date on which the trust assumes all its functions) and the accounting date of the trust (article 5), the trust’s limited functions before the operational date (article 6) and the trust’s liabilities which will be discharged by the West Hertfordshire Health Authority if incurred between the establishment date and the operational date of the trust (article 7).

items.title: The Mount Vernon and Watford Hospitals and the St. Albans and Hemel Hempstead National Health Service Trusts (Dissolution) Order 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the dissolution on 1st April 2000 of the Mount Vernon and Watford Hospitals and the St. Albans and Hemel Hempstead National Health Service Trusts.

items.title: The Road Traffic Reduction Act 1997 (Commencement) (England and Wales) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into force the Road Traffic Reduction Act 1997, as regards to England and Wales, on 10th March 2000.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Amendment No. 3)Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations prescribe circumstances in which section 19A(1) of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992, is not to apply. That provision prevents refunds being made in respect of Class 1, 1A and 1B contributions, which have been paid due to an error as to the employment status of the earner, more than two years after the payment of relevant earnings except in prescribed circumstances.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Amendment No. 3) (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations prescribe circumstances in which section 19A(1) of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992, is not to apply. That provision prevents refunds being made in respect of Class 1, 1A and 1B contributions, which have been paid due to an error as to the employment status of the earner, more than two years after the payment of relevant earnings except in prescribed circumstances.

items.title: The European Communities (Designation) Order 2000 (revoked)
items.description: This is a further Order in the series designating authorities which may exercise the power to make regulations conferred by section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972 and specifying the matters in relation to which that power may be exercised.

items.title: The Parliamentary Commissioner Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends Schedule 2 to the Parliamentary Commissioner Act 1967 by adding the Disability Rights Commission to the list of bodies which are subject to investigation by the Parliamentary Commissioner for Administration.

items.title: The Films Co-Production Agreements (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: The Films Co-Production Agreements Order 1985 (“the principal Order ”) provides that films made in accordance with the agreements between the Government of the United Kingdom and the Governments of the countries listed in the Schedule, as substituted by S.I. 1994/3222, are to be treated as British Films for the purposes of Schedule 1 to the Films Act 1985. The present Order corrects the Command Paper reference in the list of agreements scheduled to the principal Order for an agreement with France of 8th November 1994 (Cm. 2992) and extends the list of agreements scheduled to the principal Order to include an agreement with Italy of 5th May 1998 (Cm. 4029).

items.title: The Northern Ireland Act 1998 (Modification) Order 2000
items.description: This Order adds the Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999 (c. 30) and the Welfare Reform and Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/3147 (NI 11)) to the legislation listed in section 87 of the Northern Ireland Act 1998 (c. 47). This legislation is subject to the provisions in that section concerning consultation and co-ordination between the Secretary of State and the Northern Ireland Department having responsibility for social security. The Department exercises that role in accordance with paragraph 4 of the Schedule to the Northern Ireland Act 2000 (c. 1).

items.title: The Chemical Weapons Act 1996 (Guernsey) Order 2000
items.description: This Order applies section 2 of the Chemical Weapons Act 1996, as it has effect in the United Kingdom, to bodies incorporated under the law of any part of the Bailiwick of Guernsey. It also extends that Act to the Bailiwick of Guernsey with the exceptions, adaptations and modifications specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Census Order 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the taking of a census for England and Wales on 29th April 2001. The Order specifies the persons by whom and with respect to whom the census returns are to be made and sets out the particulars to be stated in the returns.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Agency Arrangements) (Specification) Order 2000
items.description: This Order specifies certain functions of the Scottish Ministers for the purposes of section 93(1) of the Scotland Act 1998. Section 93(1) allows a Minister of the Crown to make arrangements for any of his specified functions to be exercised on his behalf by the Scottish Ministers and allows the Scottish Ministers to make arrangements forany of their specified functions to be exercised on their behalf by a Minister of the Crown.

items.title: The Scotland Act 1998 (Cross-Border Public Authorities) (Forestry Commissioners) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, which is made under section 89 of the Scotland Act 1998 (“the 1998 Act”), amends statutory provisions relating to the Forestry Commissioners, who are a cross-border public authority for the purposes of that Act. The Order makes further amendments to provisions which govern the funding of forestry functions, following the abolition of the Forestry Fund on the coming into force of the 1998 Act in July 1999. It supersedes certain amendments made by the Scotland Act 1998 (Cross-Border Public Authorities) (Adaptation of Functions etc.) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/1747) (“the 1999 Order”). This Order comes into force on the day after the day on which it is made.

items.title: The Social Security Contributions (Notional Payment of Primary Class 1 Contribution) Regulations 2000
items.description: Section 6A of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 (c. 4) (inserted by paragraph 3 of Schedule 9 to the Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999 (c. 30)) (“section 6A”) provides that, subject to any prescribed exceptions or modifications, a primary Class 1 contribution shall be treated, for any purposes relating to contributory benefits, as having been paid where in any tax week a payment of earnings to an earner in respect of his employment is not less than the current lower earnings limit but does not exceed the current primary threshold. These Regulations prescribe exceptions and modifications and also make consequential amendments to the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (S.I. 1979/591) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security Contributions (Notional Payment of Primary Class 1 Contribution) (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2000
items.description: Section 6A of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (c. 7) (inserted by paragraph 3 of Schedule 10 to the Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999 (c. 30)) (“section 6A”) provides that, subject to any prescribed exceptions or modifications, a primary Class 1 contribution shall be treated, for any purposes relating to contributory benefits, as having been paid where in any tax week a payment of earnings to an earner in respect of his employment is not less than the current lower earnings limit but does not exceed the current primary threshold. These Regulations prescribe exceptions and modifications and also make consequential amendments to the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1979 (S.R. 1979 No. 186) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Incentive Payments) Amendment Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Incentive Payments) Regulations 1987 (“the Regulations”) which make provision in connection with, among other things, the payments made under section 7 of the Social Security Act 1986 to the trustees or managers of occupational pension schemes which became contracted-out between 1986 and 1993. They also amend the Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Incentive Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the Northern Ireland Regulations”) which make provision in connection with the equivalent payments under Article 9 of the Social Security (Northern Ireland) Order 1986. Functions exercisable by the Secretary of State under the Regulations, or by the former Department of Health and Social Services for Northern Ireland under the Northern Ireland Regulations, were transferred to the Commissioners of Inland Revenue with effect from 1st April 1999.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Contracting-out) (Payment and Recovery of Remaining Balances)Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations are made in exercise of powers inserted in sections 41 and 42A of the Pension Schemes Act 1993, and in sections 37 and 38A of the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993, by Schedules 9 and 10 respectively to the Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999. As the Regulations are accordingly consequential upon enactments contained in that Act, and as they are made no later than six months after the coming into force of those enactments, the Regulations are not subject to the requirement for consultation under section 185 of the Pension Schemes Act 1993.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Re-rating and National Insurance Funds Payments) Order 2000
items.description: Following the transfer of National Insurance functions and associated functions from the Secretary of State and the Department of Health and Social Services for Northern Ireland to the Treasury, effected by the Social Security Contributions (Transfer of Functions, etc.) Act 1999 (1999 c. 2) and the Social Security Contributions (Transfer of Functions, etc.) (Northern Ireland) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/671), the opportunity is being taken to combine the relevant annual changes to National Insurance Contributions for Great Britain and Northern Ireland in a single instrument.

items.title: The Films (Modification of the Definitionof British Film) Order 2000
items.description: This Order modifies the definition of “British film” in Schedule 1 to the Films Act 1985, as modified by the Films (Modification of the Definition of “British Film”) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/2386) (“the 1999 Order”).

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Re-rating) Consequential Amendment (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend regulation 96 of the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1979 (which relates to share fishermen). The Regulations amend paragraph (c) of that regulation by altering the special rate of any Class 2 contributions payable by share fishermen from £7.20 to £2.65.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Amendment No. 4) (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1979 (S.R. 1979 No. 186) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Charitable Deductions (Approved Schemes) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Charitable Deductions (Approved Schemes) Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/2211) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Re-rating) Consequential Amendment Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend regulation 98 of the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (which relates to share fishermen). The Regulations amend paragraph (c) of that regulation by altering the special rate of any Class 2 contributions payable by share fishermen from £7.20 to £2.65.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Amendment No. 4) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (S.I. 1979/591) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Genetically Modified and Novel Foods (Labelling) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England only, make continued provision for the enforcement of–

items.title: The A205 Trunk Road (Lewisham) Red Route Traffic Order 1998 Variation Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The A205 Trunk Road (Lewisham) Red Route Traffic Order 1998 Variation Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Rail Vehicle Accessibility (Gatwick Express Class 460 Vehicles) Exemption Order 2000
items.description: This Order authorises the use of specified rail vehicles forming Class 460 electrical multiple-units, although they do not confirm with certain requirements of the Rail Vehicle Accessibility Regulations 1998 because:

items.title: The Access to Justice Act 1999 (Commencement No. 3, Transitional Provisions and Savings) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings the following provisions of the Access to Justice Act 1999 into force on 1st April 2000: sections 1 to 11 (Legal Services Commission and Community Legal Service), 19 (services relating to foreign law), 20 (disclosure of information), 21 (misrepresentation), 22 (relationship between service providers and other parties), 23 (Lord Chancellor’s guidance), 25 (orders, regulations and directions), 26 (interpretation), 27 (conditional fee agreements), 29 and 30 (costs). Those sections which relate both to civil and to criminal matters are brought into force regarding civil matters only.

items.title: The Marketing Authorisations for Veterinary Medicinal Products Amendment Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Marketing Authorisations for Veterinary Medicinal Products Regulations 1994 so as to implement:

items.title: The A205 Trunk Road (Greenwich) Red Route Traffic Order 1998 Variation Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The A205 Trunk Road (Greenwich) Red Route Traffic Order 1998 Variation Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A4 Trunk Road (Hillingdon and Hounslow) Red Route Traffic Order 1997 Variation Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The A4 Trunk Road (Hillingdon and Hounslow) Red Route Traffic Order 1997 Variation Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Health Act 1999 (Commencement No. 8) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into force further provisions of the Health Act 1999.

items.title: The London Borough of Barking and Dagenham (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the London borough of Barking and Dagenham. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The London Borough of Croydon (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect, subject to one modification, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the London borough of Croydon. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002. The modification relates to the name of one of the proposed new wards. The Secretary of State has decided that the ward for which the Commission suggested the name “Selsdon” should be called “Selsdon and Ballards”.

items.title: The London Borough of Hackney (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the London borough of Hackney. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The London Borough of Haringey (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the London borough of Haringey. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The London Borough of Islington (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect, subject to one modification, to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the London borough of Islington. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002. The modification relates to the name of one of the proposed new wards. The Secretary of State has decided that the ward for which the Commission suggested the name “Hillmarton with Holloway” should be called “Holloway”.

items.title: The Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order give effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The London Borough of Southwark(Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the London borough of Southwark. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The London Borough of Tower Hamlets (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the London borough of Tower Hamlets. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The City of Westminster (Electoral Changes) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to recommendations by the Local Government Commission for England for electoral changes in the City of Westminster. The changes have effect in relation to local government elections to be held on and after 2nd May 2002.

items.title: The Greater London Authority Elections (Expenses) Order 2000
items.description: This Order prescribes the maximum expenditure which may be incurred at Greater London Authority elections by candidates or their agents (article 3) and by other persons (article 2).

items.title: The Meat Products (Hygiene) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations extend to England only. They amend–the Meat Products (Hygiene) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/3082, as already amended);the Meat Products (Hygiene) (Amendment) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/683); andthe Products of Animal Origin (Import and Export) Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/3124, as already amended),in each case in so far as they extend to England (S.I. 1994/3082, S.I. 1999/683 and S.I. 1996/3124 all extend to the whole of Great Britain).

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (Metropolitan Borough of Sandwell) Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates the Metropolitan Borough of Sandwell, excluding the M5 Motorway and the M6 Motorway, together with their on and off slip roads, for the whole of their lengths in the Metropolitan Borough, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 22nd March 2000, further amend the Value Added Tax Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/2518) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Tax Credits Schemes (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations 1991 (S.I. 1991/2887) and the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1973) (collectively “the principal Regulations”). Disability working allowance and family credit became known as disabled person’s tax credit and working families' tax credit respectively (collectively “tax credits”) on 5th October 1999.

items.title: The Tax Credits Schemes (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Disability Working Allowance (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (S.R. 1992 No. 78) and the Family Credit (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (S.R. 1987 No. 463) (collectively “the principal Regulations”). Disability working allowance and family credit became known as disabled person’s tax credit and working families' tax credit respectively (collectively “tax credits”) on 5th October 1999.

items.title: The Commission for Health Improvement (Functions) Amendment Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Commission for Health Improvement (Functions) Regulations 2000 to correct errors in the cross-references to other provisions of the Regulations in regulation 20(5) (requiring a person to put information in a form in which the identity of an individual cannot be identified) and regulation 22 (exercising functions in relation to health service inquiries).

items.title: The National Assistance (Sums for Personal Requirements) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations set out the weekly sum which local authorities in England are to assume, in the absence of special circumstances, that residents in accommodation arranged under Part III of the National Assistance Act 1948 will need for their personal requirements.

items.title: The Social Security (Overlapping Benefits) Amendment Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Overlapping Benefits) Regulations 1979 (S.I. 1979/597) by altering the amount of the adjustment of benefits payable under the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 by reference to the new rates of child benefit payable under the Social Security Benefits Up-rating Order 2000. They provide for the commencement of the adjustment to coincide with the commencement of that Order. They also make a minor drafting correction.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (Metropolitan Borough of Sefton) Order 2000
items.description: This Order designates the Metropolitan Borough of Sefton, excluding those lengths of road described in Schedule 1, as both a permitted parking area and a special parking area in accordance with Schedule 3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991. It also applies with modifications various provisions of Part II of that Act to the designated area and modifies the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 in relation to the designated area.

items.title: The Pollution Prevention and Control Act 1999 (Commencement No. 1) (England and Wales) Order 2000
items.description: Article 2 brings into force on 21st March 2000 section 6(1) of and Schedule 2 to the Pollution Prevention and Control Act 1999 in so far as they extend to England and Wales.

items.title: The Greater London Authority Act 1999 (Commencement No. 4 and Adaptation) Order 2000
items.description: Article 2 of this Order brings into force certain provisions of the Greater London Authority Act 1999. Provisions relating to the metropolitan police district, additional to those already in force, come into force on 1st April 2000. On that date the London Advisory Planning Committee, the London Ecology Unit and the London Research Centre cease to exist.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Fund-Raising Events by Charities and Other Qualifying Bodies) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st April 2000, substitutes a new Group 12 of Schedule 9 to the Value Added Tax Act 1994 (c. 23) (exemption for fund-raising events by charities and other qualifying bodies).

items.title: The Inheritance Tax (Indexation) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, made under section 8 of the Inheritance Tax Act 1984 (formerly the Capital Transfer Tax Act 1984) (“section 8”), substitutes a new Table of rate bands and rates in Schedule 1 to that Act for the Table which was substituted by the Inheritance Tax (Indexation) Order 1999 (S.I. 1999/596) in relation to chargeable transfers made in the year beginning 6th April 1999.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Increase of Registration Limits) Order 2000
items.description: This Order increases the VAT registration limits for taxable supplies and for acquisitions from other member States from £51,000 to £52,000, with effect from 1st April 2000.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Charities and Aids for the Handicapped) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st April 2000, amends Groups 12 and 15 of Schedule 8 to the Value Added Tax Act 1994 (c. 23) (“the Act”).

items.title: The Income Tax (Indexation) Order 2000
items.description: Sections 1(6) and 257C(3) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“the Act”) operate to provide that the Treasury shall by order made by statutory instrument before 6th April 2000 specify the amounts which by virtue of those sections shall, unless Parliament otherwise determines, be treated as specified for the year 2000–01.

items.title: The Retirement Benefits Schemes (Indexation of Earnings Cap) Order 2000
items.description: Section 590C(1) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“the Act”) provides that in arriving at an employee’s final remuneration for the purposes of section 590(3)(a) or (d) of the Act (conditions for approval of retirement benefits schemes), any excess over the permitted maximum figure (“the earnings cap”) for the year of assessment in which the employee’s participation in the scheme ceases shall be disregarded.

items.title: The Capital Gains Tax (Annual Exempt Amount) Order 2000
items.description: This Order specifies £7,200 as the amount which, under section 3 of the Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992 (“the 1992 Act”), is the exempt amount for the year 2000–01 unless Parliament otherwise determines. Section 3(1) of the 1992 Act provides that an individual’s capital gains for a year of assessment are chargeable to capital gains tax only to the extent that they exceed the exempt amount.

items.title: The Individual Savings Account (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations which come into force on 6th April 2000, amend the Individual Savings Account Regulations 1998 (S.I. 1998/1870, amended by S.I. 1998/3174, but not relevantly to these Regulations). They provide that the annual subscription limits for (i) “maxi-accounts” including the maximum which may be allocated out of that amount to a cash component, and (ii) “mini-accounts” made up of a cash component, are to remain the same in the year of assessment 2000–01 as they were in the year of assessment 1999–00.

items.title: The Income Tax (Cash Equivalents of Car Fuel Benefits) Order 2000
items.description: This Order provides for new tables of flat rate cash equivalents to be substituted in section 158(2) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 with effect from 6th April 2000. Directors and employees earning £8,500 or more a year for whom fuel is provided for private use in a company car are chargeable to income tax on an amount equal to the appropriate cash equivalent of the benefit.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Increase of Consideration for Fuel) Order 2000
items.description: VAT is payable if road fuel of a business is used for private motoring. The VAT due must be calculated using flat-rate values related to engine type and size.

items.title: The Community Legal Service (Scope) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 2 to the Access to Justice Act 1999 in order to provide that:

items.title: The Conditional Fee Agreements Order 2000
items.description: Under sections 58 and 58A of the Courts and Legal Services Act 1990, all proceedings may be the subject of an enforceable fee agreement except specified family proceedings and criminal proceedings other than those under section 82 of the Environmental Protection Act 1990. This Order specifies the proceedings to which a conditional fee agreement must relate if it is to provide for a success fee. These are all proceedings which may be the subject of a conditional fee agreement except criminal proceedings under section 82 of the Environmental Protection Act 1990.

items.title: The Community Legal Service (Cost Protection) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations also contain provisions relating to the enforcement of an order for costs against a funded client.

items.title: The Greater London Authority Election (Early Voting) Order 2000
items.description: This Order specifies the days and times for early voting in the first ordinary election of the Greater London Authority and, by reference to electoral areas, the polling stations at which early voting may take place. The Order also makes some consequential modifications of the legislation governing elections for the purposes of the Greater London Authority.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Quota and Third Country Fishing Measures) Order 2000
items.description: This Order makes provision for the enforcement of certain enforceable Community restrictions and other obligations relating to sea fishing by both Community and third country vessels set out in Council Regulation (EC) No. 2742/1999 (OJ No. L341, 31.12.99, p.1) (“the Council Regulation”). The Council Regulation fixes total allowable catches and member States' quotas for 2000 and lays down certain conditions under which they may be fished. It also authorises fishing by vessels of Norway and the Faroe Islands for specified descriptions of fish in certain specified areas within member States' fishery limits in 2000 and imposes requirements concerning fishing quotas and authorised zones, methods of fishing, the holding of licences and observance of licence conditions, the keeping of log books, the making of reports and similar matters.

items.title: The Agricultural or Forestry Tractors and Tractor Components (Type Approval) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: The Regulations, which apply throughout the United Kingdom, consolidate and further amend Schedule 2 to the Agricultural or Forestry Tractors and Tractor Components (Type Approval) Regulations 1988 which specifies the Community directives establishing type approval requirements with respect to the design, construction, equipment and marking of tractors and tractor components. The Regulations implement on the dates specified in regulation 4–

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) Red Route (Bus Priority) Traffic Order 1999 Experimental Variation Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) Red Route (Bus Priority) Traffic Order 1999 Experimental Variation Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Environmental Protection (Waste Recycling Payments) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: Section 52(1) of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 requires waste disposal authorities to pay waste collection authorities amounts representing their net savings on the disposal of waste retained by collection authorities for recycling.

items.title: The Income Support (General) (Standard Interest Rate Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 3 to the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/1967), as substituted and amended, with respect to the standard rate of interest applicable to a loan which qualifies for income support under that Schedule to those Regulations. The new rate is 6.92 per cent. (regulation 2). The Regulations also revoke, with a saving provision, regulations which made a previous amendment to that standard rate of interest (regulation 3).

items.title: The Occupational and Personal Pension Schemes (Penalties) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations are made under the Pension Schemes Act 1993 and the Pensions Act 1995.

items.title: Prevention of Terrorism (Temporary Provisions) Act 1989 (Continuance) Order 2000
items.description: This Order continues in force for a period of 12 months from 22nd March 2000 the provisions of–

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Training and Certification) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Merchant Shipping (Training and Certification) Regulations 1997. They implement amendments to the International Convention on Standards of Training, Certification and Watchkeeping for Seafarers 1978 (the STCW Convention), as amended in July 1995 (STCW 95). These amendments were adopted by the Maritime Safety Committee of the International Maritime Organization at its 68th session in May/June 1997, by resolutions MSC.66(68) and MSC.67(68).

items.title: The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations are made to correct an omission from the National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) Amendment Regulations 2000.

items.title: The Education Maintenance Allowance (Pilot Areas) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 8 of the Education Maintenance Allowance (Pilot Areas) Regulations 1999.

items.title: The Electricity (Standards of Performance) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Electricity (Standards of Performance) Regulations 1993 as amended by the Electricity (Standards of Performance) (Amendment) Regulations 1995 and the Electricity (Standards of Performance) (Amendment) Regulations 1998 (“the Principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Stockport Acute Services and the Stockport Healthcare National Health Service Trusts (Dissolution) Order 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the dissolution on 1st April 2000 of the Stockport Acute Services and the Stockport Healthcare National Health Service Trusts.

items.title: The Stockport National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the Stockport National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust (article 3) and the number of executive and non-executive directors (article 4). It specifies the operational date (the date on which the trust assumes all its functions) and the accounting date of the trust (article 5).

items.title: The Police Pensions (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Pensions Regulations 1987 (“the 1987 Regulations”) with respect to unpaid maternity and sick leave. Regulation 1 provides for the amendments relating to unpaid maternity leave to have effect as from 10th June 1994 and the amendments relating to unpaid sick leave to have effect from 1st September 1995.

items.title: Charities (Boxmoor Estate, Hemel Hempstead) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to a Scheme of the Charity Commissioners for the Charity presently known as the Boxmoor Estate. The Charity is of ancient origin being founded in 1594 and is now regulated by the Boxmoor Act 1809 (“the Act”) and various Schemes of the Charity Commissioners (“the old Schemes ”).

items.title: The Medical Food (England) Regulations 2000 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st November 2001, implement in England Commission Directive 1999/21/EC on dietary foods for special medical purposes.

items.title: The Phoenix National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2000
items.description: This Order dissolves the Phoenix National Health Service Trust on 1st April 2000.

items.title: The Andover District Community Health Care National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2000
items.description: This Order provides for the dissolution on 1st April 2000 of the Andover District Community Health Care National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The Relevant Functions (Payments to Authorities) Order 2000
items.description: This Order sets out the process by which the Secretary of State will make payments to local authorities in respect of expenses incurred in connection with the carrying out of any “relevant function” as defined in section 140EE of the Social Security Administration Act 1992. Relevant functions are processing claims as permitted by the Social Security (Claims and Information) Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/3108) and conducting work-focused interviews.

items.title: The Trafford Park Railway Order 2000
items.description: This Order is supplemental to the West Manchester Light Railways Orders 1899 to 1906, the Trafford Park Acts 1904 to 1922 and the Trafford Park Railway Order 1995.

items.title: The M11 Motorway (Junction 5–4, Redbridge) (Speed Limit) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations impose a speed limit of 50 miles per hour on a length of the M11 Motorway in the London Borough of Redbridge. The length affected is 3.2 kilometres long, and runs along the southbound main carriageway of the Motorway from a point 260 metres south of the centre point of its junction with Luxborough Lane to its junction with the southbound and westbound carriageway of the A406 North Circular Road.

items.title: The Leeds Teaching Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2000
items.description: This Order transfers trust property from the Leeds Teaching Hospitals National Health Service Trust to the trustees established for that trust under section 11(1) of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 and makes related provision. A copy of the schedule of trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Financial and Audit Support Branch, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The University College London Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2000
items.description: This Order transfers trust property from the University College London Hospitals National Health Service Trust to the trustees established for that trust under section 11(1) of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 and makes related provision. A copy of the schedule of trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Financial and Audit Support Branch, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Hammersmith Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 2000
items.description: This Order transfers trust property from the Hammersmith Hospitals National Health Service Trust to the trustees established for that trust under section 11(1) of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 and makes related provision. A copy of the schedule of trust property transferred under this Order can be obtained from the Department of Health, Financial and Audit Support Branch, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Greater London Authority Act 1999 (Transitional Capital Finance Provisions) Order 2000
items.description: This Order contains transitional provisions which are required because the Greater London Authority and functional bodies constituted under the Greater London Authority Act 1999 will come into existence and assume their powers part way through the financial year beginning on 1st April 2000.

items.title: The Action for Education and Employment South East Sheffield Education Action Zone Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the Action for Education and Employment South East Sheffield Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Derby North East Education Action Zone Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the Derby North East Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Bristol Education Action Zone Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the Bristol Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The North Stockton Community Education Action Zone Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the North Stockton Community Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. The Order also provides (in articles 4, 5 and 6) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Great Yarmouth Achievement Education Action Zone Order 2000
items.description: This Order establishes the Great Yarmouth Achievement Education Action Zone, which comprises the schools listed in Schedule 2. This Order also provides (in articles 4 and 5) for the membership of the Education Action Forum for the zone.

items.title: The Education (School Teachers' Pay and Conditions) Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the School Teachers' Pay and Conditions Document 1999 by uprating the amounts of salary and certain allowances. The following are increased—

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (EC Type Approval) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (EC Type Approval) Regulations 1998.

items.title: The Health Service Medicines(Price Control Appeals) Amendment Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations correct errors in the Health Service Medicines (Price Control Appeals) Regulations 2000 (No. 124) (“the principal Regulations”). They are being issued free to all known recipients of the principal Regulations.

items.title: The A4 Trunk Road (Great West Road and Boston Park Road, Hounslow) (Prohibition of Traffic Movements) Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The A4 Trunk Road (Great West Road and Boston Park Road, Hounslow) (Prohibition of Traffic Movements) Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A4 Trunk Road (Great West Road and Boston Manor Road, Hounslow) (Prohibition of Traffic Movements) Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The A4 Trunk Road (Great West Road and Boston Manor Road, Hounslow) (Prohibition of Traffic Movements) Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A4 Trunk Road (Great West Road and Riverbank Way, Hounslow) (Prescribed Routes) Order 2000
items.description: This version of the The A4 Trunk Road (Great West Road and Riverbank Way, Hounslow) (Prescribed Routes) Order 2000 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Lobsters and Crawfish (Prohibition of Fishing and Landing) Order 2000
items.description: This Order prohibits fishing for, and landing of, lobsters and crawfish bearing a V notch or mutilated in such a manner as to obscure a V notch by both relevant British fishing boats and Scottish fishing boats (Articles 3 and 4).

items.title: The Employment Relations Act 1999 (Commencement No. 5 and Transitional Provision) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings into force, on 24th April 2000, section 16 of and Schedule 5 to the Employment Relations Act 1999. These provisions amend Part V of the Trade Union and Labour Relations (Consolidation) Act 1992, to provide that an employee is regarded as unfairly dismissed for the purposes of Part X of the Employment Rights Act 1996 if dismissed, in certain circumstances, because he or she took part in official industrial action. The provisions also make consequential amendments to section 105 of the Employment Rights Act 1996 (unfair selection for redundancy). Article 3 of the Order provides that the changes apply only to the dismissal of employees who start to take industrial action beginning on or after 24th April 2000.

items.title: The South Peterborough Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the South Peterborough Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2000 (“the principal Order”) in order to add areas omitted in error from the principal Order.

items.title: The North Peterborough Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 2000
items.description: This Order amends the North Peterborough Primary Care Trust (Establishment) Order 2000 (“the principal Order”) in order to add areas omitted in error from the principal Order.

items.title: The Milk Development Council (Amendment) Order 2000
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st April 2000, amends the Milk Development Council Order 1995 (S.I. 1995/356).

items.title: Disability Rights Commission (Time Limits) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations supplement Part I of Schedule 3 to the Disability Rights Commission Act 1999 by making provision for time limits in relation to the conduct of formal investigations by the Disability Rights Commission (regulation 2), and prescribe the periods at the end of which action plans become final under Part III of Schedule 3 to the Act (regulation 3).

items.title: The Disability Rights Commission Act 1999 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provision) Order 2000
items.description: This Order brings certain provisions of the Disability Rights Commission Act 1999 into force on 23rd March 2000 and 25th April 2000 respectively.

items.title: The Primary Care Trusts (Membership, Procedure and Administration Arrangements) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations make provision concerning the membership and procedure of Primary Care Trusts in England, established under the National Health Service Act 1977 as amended by the Health Act 1999.

items.title: The Charities (Corporation of the Hall of Arts and Sciences) Order 2000
items.description: This Order gives effect to a Scheme of the Charity Commissioners for the Charity known as the Corporation of the Hall of Arts and Sciences (“the Corporation”) which is regulated by a Royal Charter of 1866, Supplemental Charters of 1887 and 1928, the Royal Albert Hall Acts 1876 to 1966, and the British Museum (Transitional Provisions) Order 1965.

items.title: The Corporation Tax (Instalment Payments) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 3 of the Corporation Tax (Instalment Payments) Regulations 1998 (“the principal Regulations”) so as to provide that a company is not a large company for an accounting period (so that the arrangements for quarterly instalment payments of corporation tax introduced by those Regulations do not apply) if the amount of the company’s total liability for that period does not exceed £10,000. The previous limit was £5,000. Under the principal Regulations the limit is proportionately reduced where the company’s accounting period is less than 12 months.

items.title: The Taxes (Interest Rate) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Taxes (Interest Rate) Regulations 1989 by providing for a reduction of 1 per cent. in the rate of interest payable by large companies on underpayments of corporation tax under the arrangements for quarterly instalment payments.

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Route Charges for Navigation Services) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Aviation (Route Charges for Navigation Services) Regulations 1999 (the “principal Regulations”) as follows:

items.title: The Local Government (Best Value) Performance Indicators Order 2000
items.description: Part I of the Local Government Act 1999 imposes requirements on local authorities and other authorities (the best value authorities listed in section 1 of that Act) relating to economy, efficiency and effectiveness in the exercise of their functions (Best Value). Section 4(1) of that Act confers a power on the Secretary of State to specify by order best value performance indicators and standards. This Order specifies performance indicators by reference to which a best value authority’s performance in exercising functions can be measured.

items.title: The Social Security (Work-focused Interviews) Regulations 2000
items.description: The Regulations contained in this Instrument are made either by virtue of, or in consequence of, provisions in the Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999 (c. 30) (“the 1999 Act”). This Instrument is made before the expiry of the period of 6 months beginning with the coming into force of those provisions; the regulations in it are therefore exempt from the requirement in section 172(2) of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (c. 5) to refer proposals to make Regulations to the Social Security Advisory Committee and are made without reference to that Committee.

items.title: The Contracting Out (Functions relating to Social Security) Order 2000
items.description: This Order makes provision to enable both the Secretary of State and local authorities to authorise another person or that person’s employees to exercise functions in relation to work-focused interviews. It also includes provisions enabling the Secretary of State to authorise others to exercise certain functions in relation to claims for housing benefit and council tax benefit and local authorities to authorise others to exercise certain functions in connection with claims for other benefits.

items.title: The Gaming Clubs (Hours and Charges) (Amendment) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Gaming Clubs (Hours and Charges) Regulations 1984 and the Gaming Clubs (Hours and Charges) (Scotland) Regulations 1984. They provide for a modern form of charging for bingo and for a more comprehensible form of notice to be displayed in bingo club premises, containing information as to the cost of play.

items.title: The Health Act 1999 (Supplementary, Consequential etc. Provisions) Order 2000
items.description: This Order makes amendments to certain enactments, and related provision, consequential upon provisions of the Health Act 1999 (“the Act”) concerning Primary Care Trusts and high security psychiatric services.

items.title: The Access to Justice Act 1999 (Transitional Provisions) Order 2000
items.description: This Order contains transitional provisions relating to the coming into force, on 1st April 2000, of sections 27, 29 and 30 of the Access to Justice Act 1999.

items.title: The Structural Funds (National Assembly for Wales) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations enable the National Assembly for Wales (“the Assembly”) to exercise in relation to Wales certain functions under Council Regulation 4253/88 laying down provisions for implementing Regulation (EEC) No. 2052/88 as regards co-ordination of the activities of the different Structural Funds between themselves and with the operations of the European Investment Bank and the other existing financial instruments (as amended by Council Regulation 2082/93) (“the 1993 Structural Funds Regulation”) and under Council Regulation 1260/1999 laying down general provisions on the Structural Funds (“the 1999 Structural Funds Regulation”). The Structural Funds concerned are the European Regional Development Fund, the Financial Instrument for Fisheries Guidance and the Guidance Section of the European Agricultural Guidance and Guarantee Fund; provision is not made in respect of the European Social Fund. The functions are:

items.title: The Teachers' Pensions (Contributable Salary) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations modify the definition of contributable salary in the Teachers' Pensions Regulations 1997 in consequence of the Education (School Teachers' Pay and Conditions) Order 2000 which, in addition to introducing revised pay scales for classroom teachers from 1st April 2000 provides for temporary adjustments to salary for the period 1st April 2000 to 31st August 2000. These Regulations disapply such temporary adjustments for the purposes of the Teachers' Pensions Regulations.

items.title: The National Health Service (Charges to Overseas Visitors) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2000
items.description: This Order is made to correct an omission from the National Health Service (Charges to Overseas Visitors) Amendment Regulations 2000.

items.title: The Housing Renewal Grants (Amendment No. 2) (England) Regulations 2000
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Renewal Grants Regulations 1996 “the principal Regulations” and the Housing Renewal Grants (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2000 (which amend the principal Regulations).

items.title: The Immigration (Passenger Information) Order 2000
items.description: This Order specifies certain information (relating to the passengers carried, or expected to be carried, by ships or aircraft) as passenger information for the purposes of paragraph 27B of Schedule 2 to the Immigration Act 1971 (as inserted by section 18 of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999).

items.title: The Channel Tunnel (International Arrangements) (Amendment) Order 2000
